title: Windows Software â†’ Linux Alternatives Manifest
description: A comprehensive manifest of conventional Windows software and their Linux alternatives, ordered by matching capability. Includes every column from B_installed_windows_software.csv plus additional common Windows software scraped from the web.

columns.name: Human-friendly product name (Windows software)
columns.linuxAvailability: Flags: Available on Linux, Not Available, Native Alternative, Available as WebApp, Windows Emulator (Wine/Proton), Linux Docker, Needs Review
columns.destType: Classification of the Linux destination: Native, WebApp, Wine/Proton, Docker, Flatpak, Snap, AppImage, CLI, Browser Extension, Hybrid

alternatives.name: Linux alternative name
alternatives.competency: Percentage rating vs Windows equivalent (â‰¥100 = Linux is better)
alternatives.mustInclude: yes/no â€” whether this should be installed on Linux
alternatives.canSync: Yes | No, manual transfer â€” whether data auto-syncs via cloud account
alternatives.notes: Additional context about the alternative
alternatives.installMethod: apt, flatpak, snap, docker, wine, manual, web, appimage, pip, npm, dotnet
alternatives.appType: Type of the Linux alternative: Native (APT package), Native (Flatpak), Native (Snap), Native (Docker container), Native (AppImage), Native (manual install), Native (Python pip), Native (Node.js npm), Native (.NET tool), Wine/Proton, WebApp
alternatives.downloadUrl: Download URL for native/wine alternatives or webapp URL for webapps

columns.install: Per-distro install descriptor used by the generator (method, flatpakId, native{apt,dnf,zypper,pacman}, arch, downloadUrl, webappUrl, dockerImage, note, review).

alternatives.name: ClamAV + Stacer + BleachBit
alternatives.notes: Combination covers 360's features: ClamAV (virus scan), Stacer (system optimization), BleachBit (cleanup). No single all-in-one suite matches 360's integration.

alternatives.name: Native Linux security stack
alternatives.notes: Linux doesn't need bloated 'total security' suites. Firewalld (network firewall), AppArmor/SELinux (application sandboxing), repo-based installs (verified software) + regular updates = comprehensive security without resource waste.

alternatives.name: p7zip / p7zip-full
alternatives.notes: The official 7-Zip for Linux. `7z` command identical to Windows. LZMA/LZMA2 compression (best ratio), AES-256 encryption, multi-volume archives, self-extracting support.

alternatives.name: Tesseract OCR + gImageReader
alternatives.notes: Tesseract: Google-maintained OCR engine (supports 100+ languages, LSTM neural network). gImageReader: GUI frontend â€” select area/import PDF â†’ OCR â†’ export as searchable PDF/DOCX/plain text. Best FOSS OCR combo.

alternatives.name: OCRFeeder
alternatives.notes: GNOME OCR suite. Import images/PDFs, auto-detect columns/blocks, OCR (Tesseract/CuneiForm), export to ODT/HTML/plain text. Clean GUI, batch processing.

alternatives.name: Paperwork
alternatives.notes: Document manager with OCR. Scan â†’ OCR â†’ tag â†’ search. Full-text search across all documents. Goal: paperless office. Good for document archival, not just extraction.

alternatives.name: OCRmyPDF
alternatives.notes: `ocrmypdf input.pdf output.pdf` â€” adds searchable OCR text layer to PDFs. Optimizes images, deskews, cleans backgrounds. Excellent for batch processing scanned PDFs.

alternatives.name: Clonezilla
alternatives.notes: Bare-metal disk/partition imaging and cloning. Supports 30+ filesystems, compression, encryption, multicast for mass deployment. Live CD/USB. The FOSS Acronis.

install.note: Clonezilla; also a bootable ISO from clonezilla.org

alternatives.name: Rescuezilla
alternatives.notes: GUI disk imaging (Clonezilla GUI). Point-and-click backup/restore of entire disks/partitions. Compatible with Clonezilla images. Much easier to use.

alternatives.name: BorgBackup + Vorta
alternatives.notes: Deduplicating, compressed, encrypted file backup. Not bare-metal imaging, but excellent for incremental daily backups with remote sync.

alternatives.name: Timeshift
alternatives.notes: System snapshot/restore (like Windows System Restore). Btrfs snapshots (instant) or rsync. Scheduled. Bootable restore from live USB.

alternatives.name: Stirling PDF
alternatives.notes: Docker-based PDF Swiss Army knife. Merge/split/rotate, OCR, sign, compress, convert (to/from Word, Excel, PPT, images), watermark, page numbers, repair. 50+ PDF operations via web UI.

alternatives.name: PDF Arranger
alternatives.notes: Desktop PDF page organizer. Drag-and-drop reorder/rotate/delete pages, merge/split PDFs, crop pages. Simple and fast. Pairs with Stirling PDF for comprehensive toolkit.

alternatives.name: LibreOffice Draw
alternatives.notes: Can open/edit PDFs (limited). Good for adding text/shapes, filling forms. Not a full PDF editor â€” text reflow is basic.

alternatives.name: Okular
alternatives.notes: KDE document viewer. PDF reading, annotations (highlight, note, freehand), form filling, digital signatures. Viewer first, light annotation only.

alternatives.name: HARMAN AIR SDK
alternatives.notes: Adobe AIR runtime is deprecated. HARMAN maintains AIR SDK for enterprise; most AIR apps have been rewritten as native/web apps. No consumer Linux runtime.

alternatives.name: Audacity
alternatives.notes: Multi-track audio editor. Recording, editing, mixing, spectrogram view, noise reduction, EQ, compression, VST/LADSPA/Nyquist plugins. Most popular FOSS audio editor. Covers Audition basics well.

alternatives.name: Ardour
alternatives.notes: Professional DAW. Multi-track recording, non-destructive editing, mixing, mastering, plugin support (LV2/VST3/LADSPA). Closer to Audition's multi-track workflow. Pay-what-you-want for Linux binaries.

alternatives.name: Tenacity
alternatives.notes: Audacity fork (after telemetry controversy). Same feature set without the tracking concerns. Forward-ported improvements from upstream.

alternatives.name: Ocenaudio
alternatives.notes: Simple multi-platform audio editor. Real-time preview of effects, efficient even with large files. Cleaner UI than Audacity but fewer features. Closed-source but free.

alternatives.name: Adobe Connect (Web)
alternatives.notes: Full functionality in modern browsers. No native Linux client needed.

alternatives.name: Jitsi Meet
alternatives.notes: Open-source video conferencing. No account needed, E2E encryption, screen sharing, breakout rooms. Self-hostable or use meet.jit.si.

alternatives.name: BigBlueButton
alternatives.notes: Purpose-built for online learning. Whiteboard, polling, breakout rooms, recording. Self-hosted or managed hosting.

alternatives.name: Zoom Workplace
alternatives.notes: Native Linux client available. Industry standard for video conferencing with large meeting support.

alternatives.name: Thorium Reader
alternatives.notes: EPUB reader with LCP DRM support (Readium LCP). Accessible (WCAG), EPUB 3 compliant, multiple themes and layouts.

alternatives.name: calibre
alternatives.notes: Full ebook library manager with built-in reader, format conversion, metadata editing, and DeDRM plugin support.

alternatives.name: Foliate
alternatives.notes: Modern GTK EPUB reader. Beautiful rendering, annotations, text-to-speech, Wikipedia lookup. DRM-free only.

alternatives.name: Koodo Reader
alternatives.notes: Cross-platform ebook reader supporting EPUB, PDF, MOBI, CBZ/CBR. Sync reading progress via cloud.

alternatives.name: Visual Studio Code
alternatives.notes: Modern code editor replacing Dreamweaver. Live Preview, Emmet (HTML/CSS shortcuts), 1000s of extensions (HTML/CSS/JS/PHP), Git integration, remote development. Much better than Dreamweaver for modern web dev.

alternatives.name: BlueGriffon
alternatives.notes: WYSIWYG web editor (like Dreamweaver's Design view). Based on Firefox's Gecko engine, CSS grid/flexbox editor, EPUB editing. Closest to Dreamweaver visual editing workflow.

alternatives.name: Brackets
alternatives.notes: Adobe's lightweight web code editor (open-sourced). Live Preview, inline CSS editing, preprocessor support. Discontinued by Adobe but community-maintained.

alternatives.name: Inkscape
alternatives.notes: Professional vector graphics editor. SVG native, Bezier curves, path operations, text on path, clones, filters, extensions. Open/save AI (Illustrator) files with some limitations.

alternatives.name: Gravit Designer
alternatives.notes: Cross-platform vector design. Modern UI, cloud sync, PDF/SVG/EPS export. Freemium â€” pro features (advanced export, symbols) require subscription.

alternatives.name: Scribus
alternatives.notes: Professional desktop publishing. CMYK, ICC profiles, PDF/X-3/Press export, master pages, linked text frames, fonts, vector tools, scripting (Python). The best InDesign alternative on Linux.

alternatives.name: VivaDesigner
alternatives.notes: Commercial DTP for Linux. Full InDesign-level features, IDML import/export (InDesign exchange format), cloud sync. Professional-grade. Free edition available (limited).

alternatives.name: darktable
alternatives.notes: Virtual lighttable and darkroom for photographers. Non-destructive RAW processing, 61 modules (exposure, color correction, denoise, lens correction, etc.), tethered shooting, masks, presets. Professional Lightroom alternative.

alternatives.name: RawTherapee
alternatives.notes: Advanced RAW processor. Superior demosaicing algorithms, floating-point processing, wavelet tools, film simulations. Slightly steeper learning curve than darktable but excellent quality.

alternatives.name: digiKam
alternatives.notes: Professional photo management (more focused on DAM â€” Digital Asset Management). Face detection, geotagging, tagging, rating, searching. Pairs with darktable/RawTherapee for a complete Lightroom workflow.

alternatives.name: ART (Another RawTherapee)
alternatives.notes: RawTherapee fork with simplified UI. Same powerful engine but friendlier interface. Good middle-ground between darktable and RawTherapee.

alternatives.name: GIMP
alternatives.notes: Full-featured image editor. Layers, masks, filters, color management, plugin system (G'MIC, Resynthesizer). PhotoGIMP plugin makes it look/behave like Photoshop.

alternatives.name: Krita
alternatives.notes: Professional painting tool (more artist-focused than photo manipulation). Excellent brush engines, HDR painting, wrap-around mode. Strong in digital painting, less in photo editing.

alternatives.name: Photopea
alternatives.notes: Browser-based Photoshop clone. Reads/writes .PSD files, layers, adjustment layers, filters, blend modes. Almost full PS compatibility. No install â€” works in any browser.

alternatives.name: Photoshop (Wine)
alternatives.notes: Adobe Photoshop CS6 runs well under Wine (older versions). CC/2024 requires heavy Wine patches (winetricks), unreliable. Not recommended for production work.

alternatives.name: DaVinci Resolve
alternatives.notes: Hollywood-grade video editor + color grading + audio post + visual effects (Fusion). Free version covers 95% of needs. Native Linux build (.run installer). Industry standard for color.

alternatives.name: Kdenlive
alternatives.notes: Multi-track video editor. Proxy editing, keyframable effects, titler, audio mixer, color scopes, project bin. MLT framework â€” Kdenlive is the best FOSS NLE.

alternatives.name: Shotcut
alternatives.notes: Cross-platform video editor. Wide format support via FFmpeg, timeline editing, filters (GPU accelerated), 4K support. Simpler than Kdenlive, faster to learn.

alternatives.name: Olive
alternatives.notes: Node-based video editor (like After Effects compositing). Still in alpha (0.2.x is stable-ish). Promising direction â€” compositor + NLE hybrid.

alternatives.name: Natron
alternatives.notes: Node-based compositor (After Effects/Nuke equivalent). OpenFX plugins, 32-bit float pipeline, rotoscoping, keying. Best FOSS motion graphics/compositing tool.

alternatives.name: Blender (VSE)
alternatives.notes: Blender's Video Sequence Editor. Basic NLE capability + full 3D pipeline (compositing, motion tracking, VFX). If you need VFX + editing in one package.

alternatives.name: Substance 3D Painter (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Adobe Substance 3D Painter has an official Linux build. PBR texture painting, smart materials, baking, 3D viewport. Available via Adobe Substance 3D subscription.

alternatives.name: ArmorPaint
alternatives.notes: Open-source PBR texture painter. Node-based materials, GPU-accelerated (Vulkan), live link with Blender. Early but promising FOSS alternative.

applications.name: Adobe XD
applications.linuxAvailability: Not Available; Native Alternative; Available as WebApp

alternatives.name: Figma (Web)
alternatives.notes: Industry-standard collaborative design tool. Vector UI design, prototyping, components/variants, design systems, developer handoff. Free tier (3 projects, unlimited files). Works in any browser. Adobe XD was effectively discontinued in favor of this.

alternatives.name: Penpot
alternatives.notes: Open-source Figma alternative. SVG-native, CSS-native (flexbox/grid layouts), components, prototyping, team libraries. Self-host via Docker or use penpot.app. First open-source design tool that truly competes.

alternatives.name: Akira UX
alternatives.notes: Native Linux UI/UX design tool. Early alpha â€” good potential but not production-ready yet. One to watch. Built in Vala for GTK.

alternatives.name: Angry IP Scanner
alternatives.notes: Cross-platform IP/port scanner with GUI. Fast multi-threaded scanning, custom fetchers (MAC, NetBIOS, TTL, hostname), export to CSV/TXT/XML. .deb/.rpm available.

alternatives.name: nmap + Zenmap
alternatives.notes: The gold standard for network scanning. `nmap -sn 192.168.1.0/24` (ping scan). Zenmap (GUI) shows topology maps. Much more powerful than Advanced IP Scanner.

alternatives.name: arp-scan
alternatives.notes: CLI ARP-level network scanner. `arp-scan --localnet` â€” very fast (uses ARP not ICMP). Shows IP, MAC, and vendor. Simple but effective for local subnet discovery.

alternatives.name: RustScan + nmap
alternatives.notes: Blazing-fast port scanner. Uses nmap underneath. RustScan finds open ports in seconds, then passes to nmap for service/version detection. 5000 ports in 3 seconds.

alternatives.name: nmap / Zenmap
alternatives.notes: `nmap -p 1-65535 192.168.1.1` â€” full port scan with service/OS detection. Zenmap GUI with visual topology. The industry standard port scanner.

alternatives.name: masscan
alternatives.notes: Internet-scale port scanner. Can scan the entire internet in 6 minutes. Used as a fast pre-scanner before nmap detailed scan.

alternatives.name: CPU-X
alternatives.notes: Exact CPU-Z clone for Linux. Shows CPU specs (name, cores, cache, frequency), motherboard, RAM (timings, SPD), GPU info. Same layout as CPU-Z. GUI + TUI (ncurses).

alternatives.name: Hardinfo2
alternatives.notes: System information and benchmarking tool. CPU/memory/GPU/storage/network details, with built-in benchmarks. Generates detailed HTML reports. Closest to AIDA64/HWiNFO.

alternatives.name: inxi
alternatives.notes: CLI system information. `inxi -Fxz` shows full system specs (CPU, GPU, RAM, storage, network, sensors). 200+ options. Excellent for remote/SSH support.

alternatives.name: lshw + lscpu + lsusb + lspci + dmidecode
alternatives.notes: Built-in hardware detection tools. `lshw -short` (all hardware), `lscpu` (CPU details), `lspci -v` (PCI devices), `lsusb -v` (USB). Always available, no install needed.

alternatives.name: GPU-Viewer (GpuTop)
alternatives.notes: GUI for GPU info (Vulkan/OpenGL details, driver version, temperature, clock speeds). The closest GPU-Z alternative on Linux.

alternatives.name: s-tui + sensors
alternatives.notes: Terminal-based hardware monitor. CPU temp/frequency/utilization graphs, stress test mode. `sensors` (lm-sensors) for CLI temperature/voltage/fan readouts.

alternatives.name: GNOME Clocks
alternatives.notes: World clock, alarm, stopwatch, and timer. Clean GNOME design, integrates with system notifications.

alternatives.name: KClock
alternatives.notes: KDE Plasma clock widget with alarm and timer functionality.

alternatives.name: Clock (GNOME Shell Extension)
alternatives.notes: Basic panel clock with alarm support via GNOME Shell extension.

applications.name: AlterEgo+Lexique
applications.linuxAvailability: Not Available; Native Alternative; Windows Emulator (Wine/Proton)

alternatives.name: GoldenDict
alternatives.notes: Multi-format dictionary reader supporting Babylon BGL, StarDict, DSL, ABBYY Lingvo formats. Can load the relevant dictionary files.

alternatives.name: Wine + AlterEgo
alternatives.notes: Run the original Windows AlterEgo+Lexique under Wine. Specific niche dictionaries may only work this way.

alternatives.name: Anaconda (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Full Anaconda distribution for Linux. Identical experience: conda, Jupyter, Spyder, 7500+ packages.

install.note: Download the Anaconda installer .sh and run it

alternatives.name: Miniconda
alternatives.notes: Minimal conda installer â€” only conda + Python + base packages. Install what you need, leaner than full Anaconda.

alternatives.name: Mambaforge
alternatives.notes: Drop-in replacement for conda using the faster mamba solver (libmamba). Compatible with all conda packages.

alternatives.name: Anki (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Linux build of Anki. Spaced-repetition flashcards with AnkiWeb cloud sync. Identical to Windows version including all add-ons.

alternatives.name: HandBrake
alternatives.notes: Best-in-class video transcoder. 300+ presets, batch queue, subtitle/audio/chapter support, live preview, GPU acceleration. More powerful than Any Video Converter and completely free.

alternatives.name: Selene Media Encoder
alternatives.notes: Modern GTK4 media converter. Clean simple UI (closer to AVC's simplicity), presets, queue. Flatpak.

alternatives.name: FFmpeg (CLI)
alternatives.notes: `ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -c:v libx264 -crf 23 output.mp4`. Converts any format, any codec. Most GUI converters (including AVC) use FFmpeg underneath.

alternatives.name: AnyDesk (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official AnyDesk Linux client (.deb/.rpm). Remote desktop with low latency, file transfer, unattended access.

alternatives.name: RustDesk
alternatives.notes: Open-source remote desktop. Self-hostable server, no relay dependencies. Very similar UX to AnyDesk/TeamViewer.

alternatives.name: Remmina
alternatives.notes: Multi-protocol remote desktop client (RDP, VNC, SSH, SPICE, NX). Swiss Army knife for remote connections.

alternatives.name: NoMachine
alternatives.notes: High-performance remote desktop with NX protocol. Excellent multimedia and low-latency performance.

alternatives.name: AnyLogic (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official AnyLogic for Linux. Agent-based/discrete event/system dynamics simulation. PLE (Personal Learning Edition) is free. Full feature parity with Windows.

alternatives.name: FreeCAD
alternatives.notes: Parametric 3D CAD modeler. BIM (architecture), FEM (mechanical), CAM (CNC machining). DWG/DXF import/export, STEP, IGES, IFC. CAD-focused, strong for mechanical+architectural.

alternatives.name: LibreCAD
alternatives.notes: 2D CAD only. DXF read/write, layers, snaps, dimensioning, measuring. Good for simple 2D drafting but no 3D, no parametric. Lighter than FreeCAD.

alternatives.name: QCAD
alternatives.notes: 2D CAD with DWG (commercial edition). Clean Qt interface, layers/groups/blocks, isometric projections, CAM. Professional edition adds DWG support and more tools.

alternatives.name: BricsCAD
alternatives.notes: Native .dwg CAD for Linux. Full 2D/3D parametrics, BIM, mechanical, Sheet Metal. Professional AutoCAD alternative with native DWG format. Commercial.

alternatives.name: Onshape (Web)
alternatives.notes: Cloud-native parametric CAD. Full-featured 3D CAD in browser, collaborative editing, version control. Free for public documents. No install needed.

alternatives.name: Blender
alternatives.notes: Full 3D creation suite. Modeling, sculpting, rigging, animation, rendering (Cycles/Eevee), compositing, VFX, video editing. Completely replaced 3ds Max in many studios. Professional-grade, massive community.

alternatives.name: Wings 3D
alternatives.notes: Pure subdivision modeler. Simple polygon modeling tool â€” good for learning 3D or quick mockups. Not a full 3ds Max alternative but lighter for modeling-only tasks.

applications.name: Autodesk Fusion 360
applications.linuxAvailability: Not Available; Native Alternative; Available as WebApp

alternatives.name: FreeCAD
alternatives.notes: Parametric 3D CAD modeler. Part Design, Sketcher, Assembly, FEM, CAM, Sheet Metal, TechDraw (2D drawings). FOSS alternative â€” less polished but surprisingly capable for the price (free).

alternatives.name: Onshape (Web)
alternatives.notes: Cloud-native CAD created by former SolidWorks team. Full parametric 3D modeling, assemblies, drawings, version control, collaboration. Free tier (public docs). Closest to Fusion 360's cloud model.

alternatives.name: Solvespace
alternatives.notes: Constraint-based parametric 3D CAD. Extremely small (~6MB), fast, excellent constraint solver. Very limited features compared to Fusion 360 â€” good for simple mechanical parts.

alternatives.name: FreeCAD
alternatives.notes: Parametric solid modeling with assembly, sheet metal, FEM, CAM. Growing fast but still far from Inventor's maturity for large assemblies and complex simulations.

alternatives.name: BricsCAD Mechanical
alternatives.notes: Commercial parametric 3D CAD for Linux. Sheet metal, assembly, BOM, 2D documentation. Native DWG. The best professional Inventor alternative on Linux.

alternatives.name: Maya (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Autodesk Maya has an official Linux build (RHEL/CentOS RPM, works on other distros with conversion). Full feature parity. The industry standard â€” if you need Maya, it runs on Linux.

alternatives.name: Blender
alternatives.notes: Covers most of Maya's capabilities (modeling, rigging, animation, rendering). Different workflow (hotkeys/philosophy) but equally capable in skilled hands. Completely free.

alternatives.name: FreeCAD (BIM workbench)
alternatives.notes: BIM modeling with IFC support, parametric walls/windows/roofs/stairs, 2D documentation. Still far from Revit's maturity â€” suitable for small-medium projects, not large AEC firms.

alternatives.name: BricsCAD BIM
alternatives.notes: Native Linux BIM solution. IFC-native, parametric components, 2D documentation. Commercial but the best Revit alternative on Linux for professional use.

alternatives.name: Blender + BlenderBIM
alternatives.notes: BlenderBIM add-on adds full IFC authoring to Blender. Parametric BIM objects, classification systems, clash detection. Growing fast â€” best FOSS BIM option, but different paradigm from Revit.

alternatives.name: GoldenDict
alternatives.notes: Reads Babylon .BGL dictionary files directly. Supports 20+ dictionary formats, scan popup, audio pronunciation, Wikipedia lookup.

alternatives.name: GoldenDict-ng
alternatives.notes: Modern fork of GoldenDict with WebKit-based article rendering, improved dark mode, and better HTML/CSS dictionary support.

alternatives.name: OBS Studio
alternatives.notes: More powerful than Bandicam. Scene composition, game/window/display capture, webcam overlay, noise suppression, virtual camera, recording + streaming simultaneously.

alternatives.name: GPU Screen Recorder
alternatives.notes: Low-overhead GPU recording (NVIDIA NVENC / AMD AMF / Intel QSV). Replay buffer saves last N seconds on hotkey. Ideal for gaming clips like Bandicam's game recording mode.

alternatives.name: SimpleScreenRecorder
alternatives.notes: High-performance screen recorder with OpenGL capture, multiple output profiles, scheduled recording. Good Bandicam alternative for general screen recording.

alternatives.name: Bing (Web)
alternatives.notes: bing.com works identically in any Linux browser. Can be installed as PWA via Chrome/Edge.

alternatives.name: DuckDuckGo
alternatives.notes: Privacy-focused search engine. Bang syntax (!w for Wikipedia, !g for Google). No tracking, no filter bubble.

alternatives.name: Google Search
alternatives.notes: Google.com in any browser. Largest search index.

alternatives.name: SearXNG
alternatives.notes: Self-hostable metasearch engine aggregating 80+ search engines. No tracking, no profiling.

alternatives.name: OpenSSH
alternatives.notes: Pre-installed on all Linux distros. ssh (remote shell), scp (file copy), sftp (interactive file transfer), ssh-keygen, ssh-agent. The gold standard.

alternatives.name: Termius
alternatives.notes: Modern SSH client with cross-device sync of hosts/keys, snippets, port forwarding GUI. Freemium model.

alternatives.name: FileZilla
alternatives.notes: GUI SFTP/FTPS client. Site manager, transfer queue, directory comparison. Good for visual file transfers only.

alternatives.name: Tabby
alternatives.notes: Modern terminal with SSH client, serial terminal, SFTP, config sync via cloud. Electron-based.

alternatives.name: calibre (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Linux build. Full ebook library manager with reader, format conversion, metadata fetcher, content server. Identical to Windows.

alternatives.name: OBS Studio + Kdenlive
alternatives.notes: OBS Studio for screen/webcam/mic capture (replaces Camtasia Recorder). Kdenlive for video editing (replaces Camtasia Editor). Separate workflow but very capable.

alternatives.name: OBS Studio + Shotcut
alternatives.notes: Alternative editing combo: Shotcut instead of Kdenlive. Shotcut has a simpler timeline but fewer effects.

alternatives.name: Kooha
alternatives.notes: Simple GNOME screen recorder. Good for quick recordings but no editing capability.

alternatives.name: ScreenPal (Web)
alternatives.notes: Browser-based screen recording + basic editing + cloud hosting. No install needed, works anywhere.

alternatives.name: BleachBit
alternatives.notes: The CCleaner equivalent for Linux. Cleans 2000+ apps, browser caches, system logs, APT cache, old kernels, thumbnails, recent docs. Shred files. CLI for scripting.

alternatives.name: Stacer
alternatives.notes: All-in-one system optimizer: dashboard, startup apps, system cleaner, service manager, uninstaller. Modern UI like CCleaner.

alternatives.name: Sweeper
alternatives.notes: KDE system cleaner. Clears web cookies, cache, history, recent documents, command history, clipboard. Simple KDE-native tool.

alternatives.name: MarvinSketch (MarvinSuite)
alternatives.notes: ChemAxon's chemical drawing tool. Java-based (runs everywhere). Structure drawing, property prediction, NMR/MS prediction, IUPAC naming. Free for academic use. .deb/.rpm available.

alternatives.name: BKChem
alternatives.notes: Simple free chemical drawing program. Bond drawing, templates, charge/radical handling. Python/Tk-based â€” simple but functional.

alternatives.name: Avogadro
alternatives.notes: Molecular editor and visualizer. 3D molecular building, geometry optimization (force fields), plugin framework, file format support (CML, PDB, XYZ). More 3D-focused than ChemDraw.

alternatives.name: Cisco Webex (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Cisco Webex native Linux client. Replaces Jabber for most enterprise Cisco deployments.

alternatives.name: Jami
alternatives.notes: Open-source SIP-compatible communicator. P2P architecture, no central server. Can connect to SIP PBXs.

alternatives.name: Linphone
alternatives.notes: Open-source VoIP/SIP client. Audio/video calls, instant messaging. Good SIP interoperability.

alternatives.name: Claude (Web/PWA)
alternatives.notes: claude.ai works fully in any Linux browser. Install as PWA for app-like experience with offline notifications.

alternatives.name: Ollama + Open WebUI
alternatives.notes: Run local LLMs (Llama, Mistral, Gemma, etc.) via Ollama with Open WebUI frontend. Privacy-first, offline capable but models are smaller.

alternatives.name: Jan
alternatives.notes: Desktop app for running local LLMs. One-click model downloads, chat interface, OpenAI-compatible API server.

alternatives.name: CMake (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Standard cmake package on all Linux distros. Identical build system, CMakeLists.txt files are cross-platform by design.

alternatives.name: Copilot (Web/PWA)
alternatives.notes: copilot.microsoft.com works fully in any browser. PWA install gives app-like experience.

alternatives.name: Ollama + Continue.dev
alternatives.notes: Local AI coding assistant via Continue (VS Code extension) + Ollama backend. Privacy-first, offline, but models are smaller and slower.

alternatives.name: Inkscape
alternatives.notes: Professional vector graphics editor. SVG-native, Bezier/Spirals/Pencil tools, path operations, gradient/pattern fills, text on path, extensions, PDF/CDR import (limited). Best CorelDRAW alternative on Linux.

alternatives.name: Krita + Inkscape
alternatives.notes: Krita handles raster/bitmap (Corel PHOTO-PAINT equivalent), Inkscape handles vector (CorelDRAW equivalent). Combined they cover the full suite.

alternatives.name: Gravit Designer
alternatives.notes: Modern cross-platform vector design tool. Similar CorelDRAW workflow for layouts and illustrations. Freemium â€” pro features require subscription.

alternatives.name: Scribus
alternatives.notes: Desktop publishing (like CorelDRAW's layout capabilities). CMYK, ICC color management, PDF/X export. Best for multi-page layouts, brochures, magazines.

alternatives.name: GNOME Disks / KDE Partition Manager
alternatives.notes: Right-click any .iso in file manager â†’ 'Open With Disk Image Mounter' (GNOME) or mount via Dolphin (KDE). Linux mounts ISOs natively â€” no special software needed.

alternatives.name: AcetoneISO
alternatives.notes: GUI ISO manager. Mount/unmount ISOs, convert between formats (ISO/BIN/NRG/MDF/IMG), create ISOs from files/CDs, encrypt/decrypt. Daemon Tools-like experience.

alternatives.name: Furius ISO Mount
alternatives.notes: Simple GUI ISO mounter. Mount multiple ISOs simultaneously, auto-mount at login, checksum verification. Lightweight.

alternatives.name: mount (CLI)
alternatives.notes: `sudo mount -o loop file.iso /mnt/iso`. The native way â€” always available, no GUI needed. Same for .img, .bin, .nrg files.

alternatives.name: DBeaver (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Native Linux build (Flatpak or .deb). Universal DB tool: PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, Oracle, SQL Server, MongoDB, Cassandra, +80 databases. Identical to Windows.

alternatives.name: DBeaver CE
alternatives.notes: Community Edition with fewer DB drivers (no NoSQL, no cloud warehouses). Sufficient for relational DB work.

alternatives.name: Beekeeper Studio
alternatives.notes: Modern SQL editor with beautiful UI. MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, SQL Server. Focus on query editing, not admin.

alternatives.name: Discord (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official native Linux client (.deb, Flatpak, Snap). Voice/video chat, screen sharing with audio (Wayland via XDG Desktop Portal). Cloud-synced.

alternatives.name: Discord (Web/PWA)
alternatives.notes: Browser version. No push-to-talk system-wide, no rich presence. Good as fallback.

alternatives.name: Docker Engine (native)
alternatives.notes: Native Linux Docker â€” no VM needed (unlike Docker Desktop on Windows/macOS). Runs directly on the kernel. Lower overhead, faster I/O, rootless mode available.

install.note: systemctl enable --now docker; add your user to the docker group

repo.apt:
install -d -m 0755 /etc/apt/keyrings
. /etc/os-release
docker_base=debian
case " ${ID:-} ${ID_LIKE:-} " in *ubuntu*) docker_base=ubuntu ;; esac
docker_suite="${UBUNTU_CODENAME:-${VERSION_CODENAME:-stable}}"
curl -fsSL "https://download.docker.com/linux/${docker_base}/gpg" | gpg --batch --yes --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg
echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/${docker_base} ${docker_suite} stable" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list

alternatives.name: Docker Desktop (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Docker Desktop for Linux is available but adds a VM layer. Not recommended â€” native Docker Engine is better.

alternatives.name: Podman
alternatives.notes: Daemonless container engine, Docker-compatible CLI (alias docker=podman). Rootless by default, systemd integration, pods (Kubernetes-style).

applications.name: Docker Engine
applications.version: N/A (hardcoded inclusion â€” replaces Docker Desktop)

alternatives.name: Docker Engine (native, no VM)
alternatives.notes: Docker Engine (docker-ce) installed from Docker's official APT repository. Runs directly on the Linux kernel â€” no VM needed (unlike Docker Desktop on Windows/macOS). Includes docker-ce-cli, containerd.io, docker-buildx-plugin, docker-compose-plugin. Service enabled + user added to docker group.

install.note: systemctl enable --now docker; add your user to the docker group

repo.apt:
install -d -m 0755 /etc/apt/keyrings
. /etc/os-release
docker_base=debian
case " ${ID:-} ${ID_LIKE:-} " in *ubuntu*) docker_base=ubuntu ;; esac
docker_suite="${UBUNTU_CODENAME:-${VERSION_CODENAME:-stable}}"
curl -fsSL "https://download.docker.com/linux/${docker_base}/gpg" | gpg --batch --yes --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg
echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/${docker_base} ${docker_suite} stable" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list

alternatives.name: Dropbox (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Dropbox Linux client (.deb/.rpm). Selective sync, LAN sync, SMART sync. Full feature parity.

alternatives.name: rclone
alternatives.notes: CLI sync tool supporting Dropbox + 50+ other backends. One-way or bidirectional sync, encryption, scheduling via cron. No GUI.

alternatives.name: Maestral
alternatives.notes: Open-source Dropbox client. Lighter than official client, GUI and CLI options. Selective sync, notifications.

alternatives.name: MakeMKV
alternatives.notes: Blu-ray/DVD ripper: decrypts and remuxes into MKV. Removes all protections (AACS, BD+). Free while in beta (key updates on forum). Paid for permanent license. The best ripper on any platform.

alternatives.name: HandBrake + libdvdcss
alternatives.notes: HandBrake with libdvdcss2 (DVD decryption library) can rip DVDs. Install `libdvd-pkg` then `apt install handbrake`. Good for DVDs â€” MakeMKV is better for Blu-ray.

alternatives.name: DVD ripper (dvdbackup + HandBrake)
alternatives.notes: dvdbackup extracts DVD to HDD â†’ HandBrake transcodes. Decrypts with libdvdcss. CLI workflow but fully FOSS.

alternatives.name: TestDisk + PhotoRec
alternatives.notes: TestDisk: recovers lost partitions, fixes boot sectors, rebuilds partition tables. PhotoRec: recovers 480+ file formats (docs, photos, videos, archives) from any media. The gold standard for data recovery.

alternatives.name: R-Linux
alternatives.notes: R-Studio's free Linux version. Recovers files from ext2/3/4, FAT, NTFS. GUI interface similar to EaseUS. Free for ext filesystems (NTFS requires paid version).

alternatives.name: ddrescue
alternatives.notes: GNU ddrescue â€” clones damaged drives to healthy media, then recovers data from the clone. Handles read errors gracefully. The best tool for failing drives.

alternatives.name: Foremost
alternatives.notes: CLI file carver. Recovers files based on headers/footers (works even when filesystem is destroyed). Good for forensics/recovery on raw disk images.

alternatives.name: GParted
alternatives.notes: GNOME Partition Editor â€” the definitive partition manager. Resize/move/create/delete partitions, format (ext4/NTFS/FAT32/Btrfs/XFS/50+). Live CD available. Pre-installed on many distros.

alternatives.name: KDE Partition Manager
alternatives.notes: KDE equivalent of GParted. Same features, Qt-based. Integrates with KDE system settings. Supports LUKS encryption, LVM.

alternatives.name: GNOME Disks
alternatives.notes: Built-in GNOME disk utility. Format, partition, benchmark, SMART monitoring, ISO writing. Simpler than GParted but covers common tasks.

alternatives.name: cfdisk / parted
alternatives.notes: CLI partition tools. cfdisk (curses-based, interactive TUI), parted (scriptable). Always available â€” no GUI needed for headless/rescue.

alternatives.name: DÃ©jÃ  Dup (GNOME Backups)
alternatives.notes: Simple encrypted backups. Incremental, scheduled, local/remote/cloud (Google Drive, Nextcloud) destinations. GNOME-integrated. Good for home users.

alternatives.name: Timeshift
alternatives.notes: System snapshot tool (like Windows System Restore + Time Machine). Btrfs or rsync snapshots, scheduled, bootable restore. Pre-installed on Linux Mint.

alternatives.name: BorgBackup / Vorta
alternatives.notes: Deduplicating backup with compression, encryption, remote sync (SSH). Vorta provides GUI. Extremely space-efficient â€” ideal for large/long-term backups.

alternatives.name: Clonezilla
alternatives.notes: Disk/partition cloning and imaging (like EaseUS disk clone). Live CD/USB. Supports 30+ filesystems, multicast for mass deployment. The FOSS Norton Ghost.

alternatives.name: Restic
alternatives.notes: CLI backup with dedup, encryption, and 15+ backends (S3, Backblaze, SFTP, Google Drive, rclone). Fast, verifiable (checksums), snapshot-based. Modern Go-based tool.

alternatives.name: Pika Backup
alternatives.notes: Modern GTK4 backup app using BorgBackend underneath. Clean UI, scheduled backups, remote backup support. Best GUI backup experience on GNOME.

alternatives.name: rsync + manual transfer
alternatives.notes: `rsync -avz /old/pc/user/ user@newpc:/home/user/` â€” transfers files with permissions, incremental, resume support. Copy dotfiles + personal data. No automated program migration.

alternatives.name: Aptik
alternatives.notes: Migrate PPAs, packages, settings between installs. Backup/restore app list + config. Commercial ($25) but purpose-built for Linux migration.

applications.name: Elgato Stream Deck / Game Capture HD

alternatives.name: StreamController
alternatives.notes: Full Stream Deck replacement for Linux. Plugins for OBS, system controls, media, scripts. Supports all Stream Deck models including XL and Pedal. Better than Elgato's Linux-deprecated software.

alternatives.name: OBS Studio + v4l2
alternatives.notes: OBS Studio with Video4Linux2 captures Elgato/AVerMedia capture cards via UVC (USB Video Class). Most cards work out of the box on Linux.

alternatives.name: Zotero
alternatives.notes: Reference manager + browser connector. Auto-capture citations from web pages/articles, organize with tags/collections, cite-while-you-write (LibreOffice/Word plugin), 10,000+ citation styles (CSL). Cloud sync. Better than EndNote for most users.

alternatives.name: Mendeley Reference Manager
alternatives.notes: Commercial reference manager with native Linux client. PDF annotation, citation plugin (LibreOffice), social features (groups, recommendations). Free up to 2GB.

alternatives.name: JabRef
alternatives.notes: BibTeX-native reference manager. Excellent for LaTeX users. PubMed/arXiv/DOI auto-fetch, duplicate detection, groups, customisable exports. Java-based.

alternatives.name: ESET Endpoint Antivirus (Linux)
alternatives.notes: ESET's official Linux client. On-access and on-demand scanning, updates. Available for servers/desktops. Enterprise-focused but works for individual use.

alternatives.name: ClamAV
alternatives.notes: Free alternative to ESET. Open-source, daily signature updates, on-access scanning. Less sophisticated detection rates but solid for Linux threat landscape.

alternatives.name: FileZilla (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official FileZilla for Linux. FTP/FTPS/SFTP client, site manager, transfer queue, directory comparison, tabbed browsing. Identical to Windows version.

alternatives.name: OBS Studio + Kdenlive
alternatives.notes: OBS Studio for screen/webcam recording (replaces FlashBack Recorder). Kdenlive for editing/trimming/captions (replaces FlashBack Player/Editor).

alternatives.name: SimpleScreenRecorder + Kdenlive
alternatives.notes: SimpleScreenRecorder is a more focused recorder (closer to FlashBack). Kdenlive for post-recording editing.

alternatives.name: VokoscreenNG
alternatives.notes: Feature-rich screen recorder with webcam overlay, magnifying glass (like FlashBack's zoom), countdown timer, system tray. Good FlashBack alternative.

alternatives.name: Foxit PDF Reader (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Foxit's official native Linux client. PDF viewing, annotation, form filling, digital signatures. Faster than Adobe Reader. .deb available from Foxit website.

alternatives.name: Okular
alternatives.notes: KDE universal document viewer. PDF, EPUB, CBR/CBZ, Markdown, DjVu, PS, CHM. Annotations (highlight, note, freehand, stamp), form filling, text selection. Pre-installed on KDE.

alternatives.name: Evince (GNOME Document Viewer)
alternatives.notes: GNOME PDF viewer. Clean UI, fast rendering, annotation support (highlight, note), form filling, digital signatures. Simpler than Okular/Foxit but solid for reading.

alternatives.name: PDF Studio Viewer / PDF Studio Pro
alternatives.notes: Commercial PDF editor (like Foxit PhantomPDF). View/edit/create PDFs, OCR, form design, digital signatures, redaction, batch processing. Java-based, cross-platform.

alternatives.name: MangoHud + OBS Studio
alternatives.notes: MangoHud = FPS counter overlay + frametime graph (replaces Fraps FPS overlay). OBS Studio = video recording/screenshot capture (replaces Fraps video capture).

alternatives.name: GPU Screen Recorder
alternatives.notes: GPU-accelerated screen recorder with minimal performance impact. Replay buffer (like ShadowPlay), FPS overlay, streaming. Better than Fraps for modern recording.

alternatives.name: Git (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Pre-installed or `apt install git`. Git was designed for Linux â€” native, faster, first-class citizen. All git commands identical.

alternatives.name: GitKraken
alternatives.notes: Professional Git GUI. Visual commit graph, merge conflict editor, built-in merge tool, GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket integration. Closed-source but excellent UX.

alternatives.name: github-desktop-plus
alternatives.notes: Community Linux fork of GitHub Desktop. Familiar UI, PR management, but lagging upstream features.

alternatives.name: Git Cola
alternatives.notes: Lightweight Git GUI written in Python. Staging, committing, branching, diff viewer. Fast and native.

alternatives.name: gitg
alternatives.notes: GNOME Git GUI. Commit history browser, staging area, diff viewer. Simple, follows GNOME HIG.

alternatives.name: Sublime Merge
alternatives.notes: Git client from Sublime HQ. Fast rendering, excellent diff/merge tools, integrated command palette. Paid but evaluation never expires.

alternatives.name: Stacer
alternatives.notes: All-in-one Linux optimizer. Dashboard, startup manager, system cleaner, service manager, uninstaller, process manager, resource monitor. Covers most of Glary's modules in one app.

alternatives.name: BleachBit + GParted + GNOME System Monitor
alternatives.notes: Combination covers Glary's tools: BleachBit (cleanup/registry-equivalent), GParted (disk tools), System Monitor (startup/processes). Use native Linux tools rather than monolithic suites.

alternatives.name: Ubuntu Cleaner + Stacer
alternatives.notes: Ubuntu Cleaner handles deep clean (old kernels, apt cache, logs) while Stacer provides the dashboard/optimizer UI.

alternatives.name: GNU grep (native)
alternatives.notes: grep is pre-installed on every Linux system. It's the original â€” GnuWin32 was the Windows port of this. Faster, always available.

alternatives.name: Google Chrome (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Google Chrome for Linux (.deb/.rpm). Identical rendering, extensions, sync. All Google services work identically.

repo.apt:
install -d -m 0755 /etc/apt/keyrings
curl -fsSL https://dl.google.com/linux/linux_signing_key.pub | gpg --batch --yes --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/google-chrome.gpg
echo "deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/google-chrome.gpg] http://dl.google.com/linux/chrome/deb/ stable main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/google-chrome.list

alternatives.name: Chromium
alternatives.notes: Open-source base of Chrome. No proprietary codecs (H.264/AAC may need extra setup). No built-in Flash/DRM (Widevine can be added).

alternatives.name: Brave
alternatives.notes: Chromium-based with built-in ad/tracker blocking, Tor private tabs, IPFS integration. Chrome extension compatible.

alternatives.name: Vivaldi
alternatives.notes: Chromium-based power-user browser. Tab stacking, built-in mail/calendar/notes, mouse gestures, extensive customization.

alternatives.name: Insync
alternatives.notes: Professional Google Drive sync client for Linux. Selective sync, multiple accounts, real-time sync, file conversion (Google Docs â†’ Office). One-time purchase.

install.note: Insync (.deb/.rpm) from insynchq.com; FOSS alt: rclone / google-drive-ocamlfuse

alternatives.name: rclone
alternatives.notes: CLI rclone with Google Drive backend. One-way sync, bidirectional, mount as FUSE filesystem. No GUI, powerful scripting.

alternatives.name: GNOME Online Accounts
alternatives.notes: Built-in GNOME integration. Mounts Google Drive in Nautilus file manager. Basic file access, no real sync engine (online-only access).

alternatives.name: Google Drive (Web)
alternatives.notes: drive.google.com in browser. Full-featured including Docs/Sheets/Slides editing. No offline file access.

applications.name: Grammarly for Windows
applications.linuxAvailability: Not Available; Native Alternative; Available as WebApp

alternatives.name: LanguageTool
alternatives.notes: Open-source grammar/style checker. Desktop app (Java), browser extension, LibreOffice/VS Code/Thunderbird plugins. 30+ languages. Premium for advanced rules.

install.note: LanguageTool: run via Docker (erikvl87/languagetool) or use the browser extension

alternatives.name: Grammarly (Browser Extension)
alternatives.notes: Grammarly browser extension works identically on Linux in Chrome/Firefox/Edge. Covers web text but not desktop apps.

alternatives.name: Grammarly (Web)
alternatives.notes: grammarly.com web editor. Paste or type text for checking. Full feature set but manual workflow.

alternatives.name: Writefull
alternatives.notes: AI-powered academic writing assistant. Language checking, sentence completion, abstract/title generation. Focused on academic/research writing.

alternatives.name: LabPlot + R
alternatives.notes: LabPlot for plots and basic statistics. R (via RStudio/ RKWard) for advanced biostatistics (dose-response curves, non-linear regression, survival analysis). Best combination for Prism-like science workflows.

alternatives.name: SciDAVis
alternatives.notes: Scientific plotting with curve fitting, statistical tests, error bars. Simpler than LabPlot â€” closer to Prism's niche.

alternatives.name: Gurobi (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Native Linux build of Gurobi Optimizer. Commercial license required (free academic licenses available). Full performance parity.

alternatives.name: HiGHS
alternatives.notes: High-performance open-source LP/MIP solver. MIT license. Best open-source performance for MIP, competitive with commercial solvers on LP.

alternatives.name: SCIP
alternatives.notes: Constraint integer programming framework. Academic license (free for research). Strong on non-linear and constraint programming.

alternatives.name: Google OR-Tools
alternatives.notes: Google's optimization suite with solvers for LP, MIP, CP, routing, network flows. Python/C++/Java/C# APIs. Apache 2.0 licensed.

alternatives.name: GLPK
alternatives.notes: GNU Linear Programming Kit. Functional LP/MIP solver, much slower than commercial solvers. Good for small/teaching problems.

alternatives.name: ClamAV + rkhunter
alternatives.notes: ClamAV (signature scanner) + rkhunter (rootkit detection). Combined provides HitmanPro-like second-opinion scan capability. Run on-demand for periodic audits.

alternatives.name: Lynis
alternatives.notes: Comprehensive security audit. Identifies malware indicators (suspicious files, modified binaries), system hardening gaps, and configuration weaknesses.

alternatives.name: Hotspot Shield (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Hotspot Shield Ubuntu/Debian client. Catapult Hydra protocol for speed. Same server network as Windows.

install.note: No official Linux client; use Proton VPN or OpenVPN instead

alternatives.name: Proton VPN
alternatives.notes: Swiss-based VPN with native Linux GUI client. Strong privacy (no-logs policy audited), Secure Core servers, NetShield ad-blocker, kill switch, port forwarding.

alternatives.name: Mullvad VPN
alternatives.notes: Extremely privacy-focused VPN. Anonymous accounts (no email), WireGuard/OpenVPN, port forwarding, multihop. Flat rate, audited no-logs.

alternatives.name: Windscribe
alternatives.notes: Native Linux CLI client. 10GB free/month, unlimited devices, R.O.B.E.R.T. domain/IP blocking, port forwarding on paid.

alternatives.name: Tor Browser
alternatives.notes: Anonymity network via onion routing. Not a VPN but provides similar IP-hiding. Much slower, not suitable for streaming. Highest privacy for browsing.

alternatives.name: IBM SPSS (Linux)
alternatives.notes: IBM SPSS Statistics for Linux. Official Linux build available via IBM. Same statistical tests, data management, output viewer. Full feature parity.

alternatives.name: PSPP
alternatives.notes: Free SPSS replacement. Same syntax (.sps/.sav files), same statistical procedures (T-tests, ANOVA, regression, factor analysis, chi-square). TUI and GUI available. Compatible with SPSS workflows.

alternatives.name: JASP
alternatives.notes: User-friendly statistics app with Bayesian and frequentist methods. Modern UI, APA-formatted output tables, meta-analysis, SEM. Easier than SPSS for most common tests. Flatpak available.

alternatives.name: R + RStudio + RKWard
alternatives.notes: R ecosystem: unlimited statistical power, 20,000+ packages, publication-quality graphics. RKWard provides SPSS-like GUI. Steeper learning curve but far more capable.

alternatives.name: uGet
alternatives.notes: Closest match to IDM. Download categories, scheduler, clipboard monitor, browser integration (uGet Chrome/Firefox extension), multi-connection, aria2 backend.

install.note: also install aria2 for multi-connection downloads

alternatives.name: Xtreme Download Manager (XDM)
alternatives.notes: IDM-like with browser media detection (downloads video from YouTube, etc.), multi-threaded downloads, scheduled downloads.

alternatives.name: Free Download Manager (FDM)
alternatives.notes: Cross-platform download manager with BitTorrent support. Browser extension, download acceleration, traffic limiter.

alternatives.name: JDownloader 2
alternatives.notes: Java-based download manager for file-hosting sites. Auto-captcha solving, link grabber, scheduler, 300+ site plugins. Heavier but very capable.

alternatives.name: aria2
alternatives.notes: CLI multi-protocol download utility. Multi-connection, segmented downloading, BitTorrent/Metalink. Very powerful for scripting but no GUI.

alternatives.name: BleachBit
alternatives.notes: System cleaner (like CCleaner). Cleans cache, logs, temp files, browser data, package manager cache. 2000+ apps supported. Root mode for deeper cleaning. GUI + CLI.

alternatives.name: Stacer
alternatives.notes: Modern Linux system optimizer. Dashboard, startup manager, system cleaner, service manager, uninstaller, resource monitor. All-in-one like Advanced SystemCare.

alternatives.name: systemd-tmpfiles + apt autoremove
alternatives.notes: Built-in Linux cleanup: `sudo apt autoremove && sudo apt autoclean` + systemd-tmpfiles cleanup timers. No third-party tools needed for basic maintenance.

alternatives.name: Ubuntu Cleaner
alternatives.notes: Simple system cleaner for Ubuntu/Debian. Clears apt cache, old kernels, thumbnails, browser caches. Lightweight, beginner-friendly GUI.

alternatives.name: Built-in kernel + fwupd
alternatives.notes: Linux doesn't need Driver Booster. Drivers are in the kernel (no separate install). `fwupd` + LVFS handles firmware updates. `ubuntu-drivers` / `software-properties-gtk` for NVIDIA/AMD proprietary drivers.

alternatives.name: ClamAV / ClamTk
alternatives.notes: Open-source antivirus engine. ClamTk provides GUI. Primarily detects Windows malware (useful for file servers/mail). On-access scanning with clamonacc. Signature DB updated daily.

alternatives.name: rkhunter + chkrootkit
alternatives.notes: Rootkit detection tools. rkhunter checks for rootkits, backdoors, local exploits. chkrootkit scans for known signatures. Linux-specific threat detection.

alternatives.name: Firejail + AppArmor / SELinux
alternatives.notes: Linux doesn't need active AV. Sandboxing (Firejail isolates apps) + mandatory access control (AppArmor/SELinux, pre-configured) + repositories (no random .exe downloads) = security by design.

alternatives.name: Built-in filesystem (not needed)
alternatives.notes: ext4, Btrfs, XFS, ZFS do NOT need defragmentation â€” they use extent-based allocation and online defrag is built into the filesystem. If you use NTFS on Linux, `fsck.ntfs` can optimize. Defrag utilities are a Windows concept.

alternatives.name: Synaptic Package Manager
alternatives.notes: Powerful graphical package manager. Search, install, remove, purge (full removal with configs). History, filters, mark-by-category. More thorough than IObit Uninstaller.

install.note: Synaptic is APT-only; use your distro's software center elsewhere

alternatives.name: GNOME Software / KDE Discover
alternatives.notes: Built-in app stores with uninstall capability. Handles APT, Flatpak, and Snap removals in one UI.

alternatives.name: BleachBit
alternatives.notes: Cleans leftover configs/caches after uninstall. Complements apt purge for complete removal. Auto-detects orphaned data from 2000+ apps.

alternatives.name: apt / dpkg (CLI)
alternatives.notes: `sudo apt purge package` (remove + delete configs), `sudo apt autoremove` (remove orphaned dependencies). The native way â€” no third-party uninstaller needed.

alternatives.name: XnView MP
alternatives.notes: Cross-platform image viewer/browser. 500+ formats, batch conversion/rename, metadata editing, lossless JPEG transforms, slideshow. Most IrfanView-like experience on Linux.

alternatives.name: gThumb
alternatives.notes: GNOME image viewer/browser/organizer. Batch operations (resize, rename, convert), lossless JPEG transforms, comments, web album export. More organizing, less viewing than IrfanView.

alternatives.name: nomacs
alternatives.notes: Fast image viewer with semi-transparent widgets. RAW support (via libraw), batch processing, thumbnail preview, LAN synchronization (view same image on two computers).

alternatives.name: qView
alternatives.notes: Minimalist image viewer. Just view images â€” no editing, no browser, no batch. Clean design, good keyboard shortcuts. The simplest image viewer.

alternatives.name: OpenJDK (Eclipse Temurin)
alternatives.notes: Adoptium/Eclipse Temurin builds of OpenJDK. TCK-tested, production-ready. Available for Java 8, 11, 17, 21, 24+. Standard `apt install` packages available.

alternatives.name: OpenJDK (Distro)
alternatives.notes: Your distro's packaged OpenJDK. `openjdk-8-jdk` on Debian/Ubuntu, `java-1.8.0-openjdk-devel` on RHEL/Fedora.

alternatives.name: Oracle JDK (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Oracle's official JDK builds for Linux (.tar.gz). Now free for production use under Oracle No-Fee Terms and Conditions (since JDK 17).

alternatives.name: Zulu OpenJDK (Azul)
alternatives.notes: Azul Zulu builds of OpenJDK. TCK-tested, wide platform support, long-term support options (commercial).

alternatives.name: VLC + ffmpeg
alternatives.notes: Codec packs are a Windows concept. Linux ships with ffmpeg/libavcodec, GStreamer, Mesa VA-API/VDPAU drivers â€” all codecs covered natively. VLC and mpv play everything out of the box.

alternatives.name: Kaspersky Endpoint Security (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Kaspersky's official Linux client. Real-time protection, on-demand scanning, network threat protection, application control. Enterprise-focused.

alternatives.name: ClamAV
alternatives.notes: Free open-source alternative. Lower detection sophistication than Kaspersky but covers base needs. Adequate for Linux's minimal threat landscape.

alternatives.name: Blender (Cycles/Eevee)
alternatives.notes: Cycles (pathtracer) and Eevee (real-time) renderers. HDRI lighting, PBR materials, denoising. Free but requires more setup than KeyShot. Import CAD files (STEP/IGES) via add-ons.

alternatives.name: LuxCoreRender
alternatives.notes: Physically-based renderer with Blender integration. Spectral rendering, light tracing, caustics. Can produce KeyShot-quality results but slower to set up.

alternatives.name: VLC Media Player
alternatives.notes: The universal media player. Plays everything (files, discs, streams, devices) without codec packs. Audio/subtitle sync, video filters, stream capture, Chromecast, DLNA.

alternatives.name: mpv
alternatives.notes: Minimalist, GPU-accelerated video player based on ffmpeg. Scriptable (Lua/JavaScript), excellent upscaling (anime4k, FSRCNNX shaders), youtube-dl integration.

alternatives.name: SMPlayer
alternatives.notes: GUI frontend for mpv/mplayer. Remembers playback position, subtitle search, equalizer, YouTube browser, Chromecast support.

alternatives.name: Haruna Video Player
alternatives.notes: Qt-based mpv frontend with modern UI. Playlist manager, YouTube support, customizable shortcuts, subtitle management.

alternatives.name: LanguageTool Desktop
alternatives.notes: Open-source grammar/style checker. Installed to /opt/languagetool with wrapper at /usr/local/bin/languagetool. Desktop app + LibreOffice/Thunderbird/VS Code/JetBrains plugins. 30+ languages.

install.note: LanguageTool Desktop: run via Docker (erikvl87/languagetool) or browser extension

alternatives.name: Steam
alternatives.notes: Native Linux Steam client with Proton (Valve's Wine fork). Thousands of Windows games run via Proton. Steam Deck compatibility ratings help identify working titles.

alternatives.name: Lutris
alternatives.notes: Open gaming platform: manage Steam/GOG/Epic/Origin games + emulators + Windows games via Wine. Community install scripts for complex setups.

alternatives.name: Heroic Games Launcher
alternatives.notes: Open-source Epic Games and GOG launcher. Manage, download, and play games from both stores with Wine/Proton support.

alternatives.name: LenovoLegionLinux
alternatives.notes: Kernel driver for Lenovo Legion hardware control (fan curves, power profiles, RGB keyboard). Replaces Lenovo Vantage/Arena hardware features.

alternatives.name: Solaar
alternatives.notes: Linux device manager for Logitech Unifying and Bolt receivers. For Lenovo wireless combos that use a single USB receiver, Solaar provides pairing, battery monitoring, and firmware updates. Works with many non-Logitech wireless receivers that share the same protocol.

alternatives.name: libratbag + Piper
alternatives.notes: libratbag provides configurable mouse DPI/button binding support; Piper is the GTK GUI. Covers many gaming mice and some wireless combos.

alternatives.name: Kernel HID drivers (built-in)
alternatives.notes: Most Lenovo wireless keyboards and mice work out of the box on Linux via standard HID kernel drivers. No additional software needed for basic functionality. Battery status via upower.

alternatives.name: fwupd + GNOME Firmware
alternatives.notes: LVFS (Linux Vendor Firmware Service) delivers firmware updates from Lenovo directly. fwupd handles UEFI, SSD, Thunderbolt, dock firmware. GNOME Firmware provides GUI.

alternatives.name: LenovoLegionLinux
alternatives.notes: Community driver for Lenovo Legion laptops. Fan control, power profiles, RGB keyboard control. Not an official Lenovo product â€” limited scope vs Vantage.

alternatives.name: System76 Firmware Manager
alternatives.notes: Firmware update tool (uses fwupd internally). Simpler GUI for firmware updates. Works with many Lenovo models.

alternatives.name: Flameshot
alternatives.notes: Feature-rich screenshot tool. Select area â†’ annotate (arrows, text, boxes, blur, pin) â†’ copy/save/upload to Imgur. Configurable shortcuts, pin screenshots to screen, OCR plugin.

alternatives.name: Spectacle
alternatives.notes: KDE screenshot tool. Rectangular/fullscreen/active window/delay capture. Annotation tools, export, copy to clipboard. Integrates with KDE sharing plugins.

alternatives.name: Ksnip
alternatives.notes: Qt-based screenshot + annotation tool. Very similar to Lightshot with numbered steps, blur, arrows, watermark. Can upload to Imgur/imgbb via plugin.

alternatives.name: GNOME Screenshot
alternatives.notes: Built-in GNOME screenshot (PrtScrn or Shift+PrtScrn). Simple but effective. No annotation â€” use with Drawing for annotations.

alternatives.name: Shutter
alternatives.notes: Classic Linux screenshot tool. Selection/window/fullscreen/tooltip capture, built-in editor, plugins for upload to 20+ services (Imgur, Dropbox, etc.).

applications.name: Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English 5th Edition

alternatives.name: GoldenDict / GoldenDict-ng
alternatives.notes: Can load Longman dictionary data files (LDOCE5 .dict/.ifo, Babylon .BGL, or MDX/MDD formats). Scan popup, audio pronunciations, full article rendering with images and CSS.

alternatives.name: dictd + dict-freedict
alternatives.notes: Dictionary server/protocol. Only uses free dictionaries (Freedict, GCIDE, WordNet). No Longman-quality content.

alternatives.name: ClamAV + ClamTk
alternatives.notes: Open-source antivirus with GUI. Signature-based detection, on-access scanning (clamonacc), automatic signature updates. Primarily detects Windows threats (useful for file servers/mail gateways).

alternatives.name: Linux security model (AppArmor/SELinux + Firewalld + repo-only installs)
alternatives.notes: Linux doesn't need Malwarebytes. AppArmor/SELinux sandboxes apps, firewalld blocks network attacks, repos prevent malware-infected downloads. Security by architecture, not signature scanning.

alternatives.name: Lynis
alternatives.notes: Security auditing tool. Scans for vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, outdated software, weak permissions. Generates hardening recommendations. Like a proactive Malwarebytes.

alternatives.name: Maple (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Maple for Linux (64-bit .run installer). Full symbolic computation, numeric algorithms, visualization, programming. Same features as Windows.

alternatives.name: wxMaxima
alternatives.notes: Open-source CAS alternative. Excellent for algebra, calculus, differential equations. Less polished UI and smaller function library than Maple.

alternatives.name: SageMath
alternatives.notes: Open-source mathematics software unifying 100+ packages (Maxima, GAP, NumPy, R, etc.). Python-based interface. Web notebook (Jupyter) or CLI. Competes with Maple/Mathematica.

alternatives.name: SMath Studio
alternatives.notes: Mathcad clone with native Linux version. Live math worksheet, WYSIWYG equation editing, units, symbolic math, 2D/3D plots, programming. Opens .xmcd (Mathcad) files. Excellent compatibility.

alternatives.name: wxMaxima
alternatives.notes: GUI frontend for Maxima CAS. Symbolic math, numeric computation, 2D/3D plots. More CAS-focused than Mathcad (no unit awareness by default). Good for algebraic heavy-lifting.

alternatives.name: Jupyter + SymPy + NumPy
alternatives.notes: Python notebook workflow. SymPy (symbolic math, â‰ˆ Mathematica/Mathcad), NumPy/SciPy (numeric), Matplotlib/Plotly (graphs). Interactive, documented, reproducible â€” different paradigm but very powerful.

alternatives.name: Cinema 4D (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Cinema 4D has an official Linux build (command-line rendering + full GUI on supported distros). Used in VFX pipelines alongside Houdini.

alternatives.name: Blender
alternatives.notes: Blender is the strongest FOSS alternative for motion graphics/VFX. Geometry Nodes (procedural), animation tools, simulation (fluid/cloth/particles), rendering. Cinema 4D's UI is more intuitive but Blender is free and very capable.

alternatives.name: .NET SDK (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Microsoft .NET SDK for Linux. `dotnet` CLI, MSBuild, NuGet â€” all identical to Windows. Cross-platform C#/F#/VB development.

install.note: or use your distro's dotnet-sdk / Microsoft repo

applications.name: Microsoft 365 / Office
applications.linuxAvailability: Native Alternative; Available as WebApp; Windows Emulator (Wine/Proton)

alternatives.name: Office for the Web
alternatives.notes: office.com â€” free browser-based Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, OneNote, OneDrive. Cloud-synced, real-time collaboration. Good enough for most users.

alternatives.name: LibreOffice
alternatives.notes: Full office suite: Writer (Word), Calc (Excel), Impress (PowerPoint), Draw, Base (Access), Math. ODF native, opens/saves DOCX/XLSX/PPTX. Best FOSS office suite.

alternatives.name: OnlyOffice
alternatives.notes: Best DOCX/XLSX/PPTX compatibility of any FOSS suite. Native Microsoft format handling. Collaborative editing via cloud. Available as Flatpak/desktop.

alternatives.name: WPS Office
alternatives.notes: Chinese-developed office suite with excellent MS Office compatibility. Ribbon UI similar to Office. Freemium â€” some features (PDF to Word, etc.) require paid.

alternatives.name: Microsoft 365 (Wine)
alternatives.notes: Running Windows Office under Wine/CrossOver. Works for older versions (Office 2016). Office 365 desktop app is very unreliable under Wine.

alternatives.name: SoftMaker FreeOffice
alternatives.notes: German-developed office suite with strong MS Office compatibility. Fast, lightweight. Free version limited to slightly older file format support.

applications.installedEdition: en-us, fa-ir, fr-fr, Apps for enterprise

applications.name: Microsoft Access
applications.linuxAvailability: Not Available; Native Alternative; Available as WebApp

alternatives.name: LibreOffice Base
alternatives.notes: Database frontend with forms, queries, reports, macros. Connects to HSQLDB (embedded), MySQL, PostgreSQL, JDBC/ODBC. Can import MDB files but complex forms/reports may not transfer cleanly.

alternatives.name: Kexi
alternatives.notes: KDE database app (Calligra Suite). Form/report designer, queries, macros. Connects to SQLite, MySQL, PostgreSQL. More polished than LibreOffice Base. MDB import support.

alternatives.name: NocoDB (Web)
alternatives.notes: Open-source Airtable alternative. Turns MySQL/PostgreSQL/SQLite into a smart spreadsheet with forms, galleries, kanban. Self-host via Docker. Modern Access alternative.

alternatives.name: Baserow (Web)
alternatives.notes: Open-source no-code database. Spreadsheet-like UI, form builder, relation linking, webhook automation. Self-host or cloud. Easier than Access for non-developers.

alternatives.name: Microsoft Edge (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Microsoft Edge for Linux (.deb/.rpm). Full feature parity including Collections, vertical tabs, sidebar (Copilot, etc.), IE mode. Minor: Kids Mode not on Linux yet.

repo.apt:
install -d -m 0755 /etc/apt/keyrings
curl -fsSL https://packages.microsoft.com/keys/microsoft.asc | gpg --batch --yes --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/microsoft.gpg
echo "deb [arch=amd64,arm64 signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/microsoft.gpg] https://packages.microsoft.com/repos/edge stable main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/microsoft-edge.list

alternatives.name: onedrive (abraunegg)
alternatives.notes: Free OneDrive client for Linux. Real-time monitoring, selective sync (by file/folder/pattern), SharePoint/OneDrive for Business support, dry-run mode. Active development.

alternatives.name: Insync
alternatives.notes: Commercial sync client (OneDrive + Google Drive). GUI interface, selective sync, multiple accounts. One-time purchase.

alternatives.name: rclone
alternatives.notes: CLI rclone with OneDrive backend. Powerful scripting, cron-scheduled syncs, mount as FUSE. No real-time file monitoring.

alternatives.name: OneDrive (Web)
alternatives.notes: onedrive.live.com in browser. Full access to files, Office integration. Manual upload/download only.

alternatives.name: OneNote (Web)
alternatives.notes: onenote.com in browser. Notebooks, sections, pages, ink/drawing, sync with OneDrive. PWA-capable. Good for existing OneNote users.

alternatives.name: Obsidian
alternatives.notes: Knowledge base on local Markdown files (git/Nextcloud-syncable). Graph view, backlinks, canvas (visual note board), plugins + community themes. Very different paradigm from OneNote but much more powerful for linked thinking.

alternatives.name: Joplin
alternatives.notes: Markdown note-taking with cloud sync (OneDrive/Nextcloud/WebDAV/etc.). Notebook/tag hierarchy, to-dos, web clipper extension, E2E encryption. Closest to OneNote for structured note-taking.

alternatives.name: Xournal++
alternatives.notes: Handwriting/PDF annotation tool. Pen/stylus input, drawing tools, PDF annotation (highlight, comment, draw), shape recognition, layers. Covers OneNote's ink/drawing capabilities.

applications.name: Microsoft Project
applications.linuxAvailability: Not Available; Native Alternative; Available as WebApp

alternatives.name: ProjectLibre
alternatives.notes: Full MS Project replacement. Gantt charts, WBS, resource allocation, earned value, PERT charts, cost tracking. Opens/saves .MPP files. Best drop-in replacement on any platform.

alternatives.name: GanttProject
alternatives.notes: Lightweight project scheduling. Gantt and PERT charts, milestones, resource assignment, task hierarchy, CSV/PDF export. Simpler than ProjectLibre.

alternatives.name: OpenProject
alternatives.notes: Web-based project management. Gantt, agile (Scrum/Kanban), time tracking, cost reporting, team collaboration. Self-host (Docker) or cloud. Free community edition.

alternatives.name: Planner (GNOME)
alternatives.notes: Simple project management for GNOME. Gantt charts, task/resources, calendars. Much simpler than MS Project â€” good for small projects.

alternatives.name: Scribus
alternatives.notes: Professional desktop publishing. CMYK, ICC color profiles, PDF/X-3 export, master pages, text frames, vector drawing. Open-source InDesign equivalent. Much more powerful than Publisher.

alternatives.name: LibreOffice Draw
alternatives.notes: Good for single-page layouts (posters, flyers, certificates). Less DTP-focused than Scribus but much simpler to use for basic publications.

alternatives.name: Canva (Web)
alternatives.notes: Browser-based design tool. Thousands of templates for flyers, brochures, cards, social media. Drag-and-drop, stock photos, cloud sync. Most Publisher users prefer this now.

alternatives.name: Microsoft To Do (Web)
alternatives.notes: to-do.microsoft.com â€” full To Do in browser. PWA-capable for standalone window. Cloud sync with Outlook Tasks.

alternatives.name: Planify
alternatives.notes: GTK4 task manager. Syncs with Todoist, Nextcloud (CalDAV). Project/task hierarchy, due dates, labels, reminders.

alternatives.name: Super Productivity
alternatives.notes: Advanced task/time tracker. Todo lists with Jira/GitHub/GitLab integration, Pomodoro timer, time tracking, daily summaries. Sync via WebDAV/Dropbox.

alternatives.name: Tasks.org (Sync)
alternatives.notes: Primarily an Android app but syncs via CalDAV/DAVxâµ. Companion workflow: use with Thunderbird Lightning or Evolution for CalDAV-backed tasks.

alternatives.name: GNOME To Do / Endeavour
alternatives.notes: GNOME-native task manager. Integrates with GNOME Online Accounts (Google Tasks, Microsoft To Do via CalDAV). Simple, follows GNOME HIG.

applications.name: Microsoft Visio
applications.linuxAvailability: Not Available; Native Alternative; Available as WebApp

alternatives.name: draw.io / Diagrams.net
alternatives.notes: Browser-based diagramming. Flowcharts, UML, network, ERD, BPMN, 1000+ shapes. Saves to Google Drive/OneDrive/Dropbox/GitHub/local. Desktop AppImage available. The best Visio alternative bar none.

alternatives.name: yEd Graph Editor
alternatives.notes: Java-based diagram editor. Auto-layout algorithms (hierarchical, orthogonal, circular, organic), excellent for complex graphs. Free but not FOSS.

alternatives.name: LibreOffice Draw
alternatives.notes: General-purpose vector drawing with basic diagramming. Flowchart shapes, connectors, dimensioning. Can open VSD files (limited). Good for simple diagrams.

alternatives.name: Dia
alternatives.notes: GTK-based diagram editor. UML, flowcharts, network, ER, electric circuits. Lightweight, fast. Looks dated but very functional â€” classic Linux Visio alternative.

alternatives.name: Microsoft Whiteboard (Web)
alternatives.notes: whiteboard.microsoft.com in any browser. Drawing, sticky notes, templates, real-time collaboration. PWA-capable. Good for existing Whiteboard users.

alternatives.name: Excalidraw
alternatives.notes: Virtual whiteboard with hand-drawn feel. Infinite canvas, collaboration, export to PNG/SVG, libraries. Open-source, self-hostable. Excellent for diagrams, wireframes, sketches. excalidraw.com.

alternatives.name: Lorien
alternatives.notes: Infinite canvas drawing app (native). Good for brainstorming/whiteboarding with a stylus. Flatpak. Works offline but no real-time collaboration.

alternatives.name: Rnote
alternatives.notes: Vector-based drawing/note-taking app. Infinite canvas, pressure-sensitive stylus support, shape recognition, PDF/image import. Good whiteboard replacement for local use.

alternatives.name: TeX Live
alternatives.notes: The standard LaTeX distribution on Linux. Full packages (`texlive-full`) or selective (`texlive-base` + needed collections). More complete than MiKTeX, better integrated.

alternatives.name: MiKTeX (Linux)
alternatives.notes: MiKTeX has a native Linux build. Familiar if coming from Windows MiKTeX â€” same on-the-fly package installation, same tools.

alternatives.name: Tectonic
alternatives.notes: Modern TeX engine â€” auto-downloads packages, produces PDF in one run. Not full TeX Live replacement (fewer packages, no XeTeX/LuaTeX). Good for CI/CD.

alternatives.name: Remmina
alternatives.notes: Multi-protocol remote desktop client: RDP, VNC, SSH, SPICE, NX, XDMCP, SFTP. Saved connections with profiles, tabbed interface. Pre-installed on Ubuntu.

alternatives.name: Tabby
alternatives.notes: Modern terminal with SSH client, serial terminal, SFTP file manager, config sync. Tabbed, splittable. Closest to the MobaXterm experience.

alternatives.name: Built-in terminal + OpenSSH
alternatives.notes: Linux terminal (GNOME Terminal/Konsole/etc.) + `ssh`, `scp`, `sftp`. The native Linux way â€” no GUI wrapper needed. Combine with Remmina for RDP/VNC.

alternatives.name: Termius
alternatives.notes: Cross-platform SSH client with cloud sync of hosts/keys/snippets. Port forwarding GUI, SFTP, team sharing. Freemium model.

alternatives.name: WindTerm
alternatives.notes: Fast SSH/Telnet/Serial client with built-in file manager. X11 forwarding, session management. Good MobaXterm alternative.

alternatives.name: OBS Studio
alternatives.notes: Professional-grade screen recording. Scene composition, audio mixer, filters, virtual camera. Much more powerful than Movavi Screen Recorder.

alternatives.name: SimpleScreenRecorder
alternatives.notes: Focused screen recorder with excellent performance (OpenGL recording). Simple UI similar to Movavi's approach.

alternatives.name: Kooha
alternatives.notes: Simple GNOME screen recorder. Screen/webcam/mic, select area or fullscreen. Very easy to use.

alternatives.name: HandBrake
alternatives.notes: The gold standard for video transcoding. 300+ presets for devices, batch conversion, subtitle/audio track selection, chapter markers. GPU acceleration (VA-API/NVENC/QSV).

alternatives.name: FFmpeg
alternatives.notes: CLI Swiss Army knife for audio/video. `ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -c:v libx264 output.mkv`. Supports every codec/format. Power user tool â€” no GUI.

alternatives.name: WinFF
alternatives.notes: GUI frontend for FFmpeg. Batch conversion with presets. Simpler than FFmpeg CLI but less powerful than HandBrake.

alternatives.name: Selene Media Encoder
alternatives.notes: Modern GTK4 media converter. Clean UI, preset system, queue-based batch processing. Flatpak.

alternatives.name: Kdenlive
alternatives.notes: Multi-track video editor with proxy editing, keyframable effects, titler, audio mixer. MLT framework â€” the best FOSS NLE closest to Movavi's simplicity.

alternatives.name: Shotcut
alternatives.notes: Cross-platform video editor with wide format support via FFmpeg. Simpler UI than Kdenlive, faster learning curve. Good for quick edits.

alternatives.name: OpenShot
alternatives.notes: Beginner-friendly video editor. Drag-and-drop timeline, titles, transitions, effects. Simplest FOSS video editor â€” closest to Movavi's ease-of-use.

alternatives.name: Flowblade
alternatives.notes: Film-style video editor with insert/overwrite editing model. Faster workflow for assembly editing. Less well-known but very capable.

alternatives.name: Firefox (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Pre-installed on most distros. Firefox Sync bookmarks/passwords/tabs/history/add-ons. All extensions work. VA-API hardware video decoding.

alternatives.name: Firefox Developer Edition
alternatives.notes: Pre-release Firefox with developer tools (Valence, WebIDE). Gets features 12 weeks ahead of stable. Separate profile.

alternatives.name: LibreWolf
alternatives.notes: Privacy-hardened Firefox fork. Telemetry disabled, uBlock Origin pre-installed, privacy-focused defaults. Still supports Firefox Sync.

alternatives.name: Teams PWA (Edge/Chrome)
alternatives.notes: Microsoft's recommended Linux approach. Install Teams as PWA from Edge/Chrome. Gets call notifications, screen sharing (via browser), chat, channels. Near-native experience.

alternatives.name: teams-for-linux
alternatives.notes: Community Electron wrapper for Teams. Desktop notifications, system tray, auto-start. Unofficial but widely used.

alternatives.name: Teams (Web)
alternatives.notes: teams.microsoft.com in any browser. No desktop notifications unless tab is focused. Screen sharing works. Simpler fallback.

alternatives.name: GOverlay + MangoHud
alternatives.notes: GOverlay: GUI for configuring MangoHud (on-screen display: FPS, temps, clocks, frametime graph). MangoHud is the Vulkan/OpenGL overlay (like RivaTuner/RTSS). MangoHud + GOverlay = Afterburner + RTSS.

alternatives.name: CoreCtrl
alternatives.notes: AMD GPU overclocking/fan control/power profiles. Similar to Afterburner's GPU tuning, but AMD-only.

alternatives.name: GreenWithEnvy (GWE)
alternatives.notes: NVIDIA GPU overclocking/fan control/power limit. Afterburner-equivalent for NVIDIA on Linux. Flatpak.

alternatives.name: LACT (Linux AMDGPU Control Tool)
alternatives.notes: AMD GPU configuration: clocks, voltage, fan curves, power caps. Modern Rust-based tool.

alternatives.name: Multipass (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Canonical Multipass for quick Ubuntu VMs. `multipass launch` â†’ instant VM with cloud-init. Lightweight, optimized for developers (vs heavy VirtualBox/VMs). Installed via Snap as APT replacement.

alternatives.name: LocalSend
alternatives.notes: Cross-platform AirDrop alternative. Local network file sharing, no internet, no cloud. Android, iOS, Windows, macOS, Linux clients. Auto-discovery on LAN.

alternatives.name: RQuickShare (Packet)
alternatives.notes: Brings Google Quick Share (Nearby Share) protocol to Linux. Send/receive files with Android devices and ChromeOS. Uses actual Google protocol.

alternatives.name: Warpinator
