alternatives.notes: Linux Mint's LAN file transfer tool. Simple, auto-discovers peers on local network. Android client available.

alternatives.name: KDE Connect
alternatives.notes: Phone-PC integration: file transfer, clipboard sync, notifications, SMS, remote input. WiFi or Bluetooth. Requires Android app.

alternatives.name: K3b
alternatives.notes: KDE's full-featured CD/DVD/Blu-ray burning suite. Data/audio/video discs, ISO creation/burning, CD ripping (with CDDB), disc-to-disc copy, verify burns. The Nero replacement for Linux.

alternatives.name: Brasero
alternatives.notes: GNOME disc burning app. Simpler than K3b â€” data/audio discs, ISO burning, disc copy, cover editor. Good for basic use.

alternatives.name: Xfburn
alternatives.notes: Lightweight Xfce disc burner. Data/audio discs, ISO burning, disc blanking, copy. Uses libburnia backend (no cdrtools dependency).

alternatives.name: ninja-build (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Standard `ninja-build` package. The build system Linux was designed for â€” ninja is native here. Same speed, same `.ninja` files.

alternatives.name: Stirling PDF
alternatives.notes: 50+ PDF tools: merge/split/rotate, OCR, sign, watermark, convert (PDF â†” Word/Excel/PPT/images), compress, page numbers, repair. Docker-based. Covers most Nitro features.

alternatives.name: LibreOffice Draw + Stirling PDF
alternatives.notes: LibreOffice Draw for content editing (text/images), Stirling PDF for batch operations (merge/convert/OCR). Combined covers full Nitro workflow.

alternatives.name: PDF Arranger
alternatives.notes: Desktop PDF page organizer. Reorder/rotate/delete pages, merge/split PDFs. Fast and simple â€” pairs well with Stirling PDF for comprehensive toolkit.

alternatives.name: Node.js (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Node.js via NodeSource or distro packages. npm/npx identical. Better performance on Linux (epoll/kqueue vs IOCP on Windows).

alternatives.name: nvm (Node Version Manager)
alternatives.notes: Per-user Node.js version manager. Install/switch between multiple Node versions. nvm install 24 && nvm use 24. Better than system package for dev.

alternatives.name: fnm (Fast Node Manager)
alternatives.notes: Rust-based Node version manager. Much faster than nvm, `.node-version` / `.nvmrc` support, cross-platform.

alternatives.name: Volta
alternatives.notes: JavaScript tool manager. Pins Node/npm/yarn per project automatically. `volta install node@24`.

alternatives.name: Bun
alternatives.notes: All-in-one JavaScript runtime + bundler + test runner + package manager. Drop-in Node.js compatible (mostly). Much faster npm installs.

alternatives.name: Notepadqq
alternatives.notes: Exact Notepad++ clone for Linux. Same look, same features: syntax highlighting (100+ languages), regex search, session restore, macro recording. Qt-based rewrite.

install.note: alt: install 'code' or a GNOME/KDE editor

alternatives.name: Geany
alternatives.notes: Lightweight IDE/text editor. Syntax highlighting, code folding, symbol browser, build system integration. GTK-based, very fast.

alternatives.name: Kate
alternatives.notes: KDE Advanced Text Editor. Multi-document, split views, LSP client (code completion), terminal panel, sessions. Very capable editor.

alternatives.name: Notepad++ (Wine)
alternatives.notes: Run the actual Windows Notepad++ 8.9.3 under Wine. Works very well â€” only minor visual glitches on dark themes.

alternatives.name: Gedit / GNOME Text Editor
alternatives.notes: Default GNOME text editor. Simpler than Notepad++, good for quick edits. Syntax highlighting for many languages but fewer power features.

alternatives.name: OBS Studio
alternatives.notes: Professional-grade screen recording and streaming. Scene composition, audio mixer, filters, plugin system, virtual camera. Industry standard for recording/streaming.

alternatives.name: SimpleScreenRecorder
alternatives.notes: Focused screen recorder. Simple UI, excellent performance (OpenGL recording), multiple output profiles, scheduled recording. Closer to oCam's simplicity.

alternatives.name: Kooha
alternatives.notes: Simple GNOME screen recorder. Record screen/webcam/mic, select area/fullscreen. No editing. Very easy to use.

alternatives.name: Peek
alternatives.notes: Animated GIF screen recorder. Select area â†’ records â†’ saves as GIF/WebM/MP4. Good for quick demos/snippets. Not full recorder replacement.

alternatives.name: OBS Studio
alternatives.notes: Industry-standard open-source screen recording and streaming. Scene composition, multiple sources (window, display, webcam, mic), filters, transitions, custom output resolutions and encoders. Far more capable than oCam for recording.

alternatives.name: SimpleScreenRecorder
alternatives.notes: Lightweight screen recorder focused on simplicity. Good video quality with configurable codecs (H.264, VP8, VP9), audio recording, and minimal resource usage. Closest workflow to oCam.

alternatives.name: Kooha
alternatives.notes: Modern GNOME screen recorder with clean UI. Supports area/window/fullscreen recording with WebM and MP4 output. One-click recording.

alternatives.name: Peek
alternatives.notes: Animated GIF screen recorder. Good for quick demos and tutorials. Records screen area to GIF, WebM, or MP4.

alternatives.name: OpenSSH (native)
alternatives.notes: OpenSSH client/server is standard on every Linux system. Pre-installed or `apt install openssh-client`. ssh, scp, sftp, ssh-keygen, ssh-agent â€” all included.

alternatives.name: OpenSSL (native)
alternatives.notes: OpenSSL is pre-installed on every Linux system. libssl + libcrypto are core system libraries. `openssl` CLI for cert/key operations identical to Windows.

alternatives.name: OpenVPN Connect (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official OpenVPN Connect for Linux. Import .ovpn profiles, GUI connection manager. Built for Linux â€” no TAP adapter gymnastics needed.

alternatives.name: NetworkManager-openvpn
alternatives.notes: GNOME/KDE system integration. Import .ovpn files into NetworkManager â†’ VPN appears in system tray/quick settings. More seamless than standalone client.

alternatives.name: LabPlot
alternatives.notes: KDE data analysis and visualization app. WYSIWYG plots (2D/3D), curve fitting (linear, non-linear), statistics (hypothesis tests, ANOVA), data import (CSV, Excel, SAS, SPSS, MATLAB). Closest Origin clone on Linux.

alternatives.name: SciDAVis
alternatives.notes: Scientific data analysis and visualization (fork of QtiPlot). Publication-quality plots, curve fitting, statistics, scripting (Python). Similar to Origin's core workflow.

alternatives.name: Veusz
alternatives.notes: Python-based scientific plotting. GUI for building plots, Python scripting, EPS/PDF/SVG export for publications. Very clean output but different workflow from Origin.

alternatives.name: Python (matplotlib + scipy + Jupyter)
alternatives.notes: Script-based data analysis. matplotlib/seaborn (publication plots), scipy (curve fitting/statistics), pandas (data manipulation). Steeper learning curve but unlimited flexibility.

alternatives.name: Thunderbird
alternatives.notes: Full-featured email/calendar/contacts client. Auto-setup for Microsoft 365 (OAuth2), Exchange via OWL add-on. RSS, chat (Matrix/IRC/XMPP), OpenPGP. Mozilla project.

alternatives.name: Evolution
alternatives.notes: GNOME groupware client. Native Exchange support (EWS), Microsoft 365 OAuth, CalDAV/CardDAV. Mail, calendar, contacts, tasks, memos. Enterprise-ready.

alternatives.name: Outlook (Web)
alternatives.notes: outlook.com / outlook.office.com in browser. Full Outlook experience, cloud-synced. PWA-capable for standalone window.

alternatives.name: Geary
alternatives.notes: GNOME email client. Modern conversation view, rich text compose. IMAP/SMTP only (no Exchange). Simpler, beautiful, less features.

alternatives.name: Pinta
alternatives.notes: Exact Paint.NET clone for Linux. Drawing tools, layers, history, 35+ effects/adjustments. Simple enough for quick edits, powerful enough for moderate work.

alternatives.name: Drawing
alternatives.notes: Simple image editor designed for GNOME. MS Paint-like UI with basic tools (pencil, shapes, text, crop, resize). .PNG/.JPEG only.

alternatives.name: KolourPaint
alternatives.notes: KDE Paint clone. Brush, shapes, color picker, selections, resize/rotate. .PNG/.JPEG/.BMP/.ICO/.XPM. The closest MS Paint clone on Linux.

alternatives.name: GIMP
alternatives.notes: Full Photoshop-level image editor. Much more powerful than Paint, steeper learning curve. Layers, filters, color management, plugin system. Overkill for simple edits.

alternatives.name: Krita
alternatives.notes: Professional painting/digital art tool. Excellent brush engine, layer management, HDR painting. More artist-focused than general image editor.

alternatives.name: Pandoc (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Standard `pandoc` package. Universal document converter â€” identical CLI, same output quality. Markdown â†” DOCX â†” PDF â†” HTML â†” EPUB â†” LaTeX plus dozens more.

alternatives.name: PaperCut Print Deploy (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Install the NATIVE PaperCut Linux client from your PaperCut server. NEVER substitute with CUPS â€” PaperCut manages print quotas, authentication, and accounting.

install.note: PaperCut Print Deploy client from your print server's portal

alternatives.name: Parsec (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Parsec Linux client (.deb/.rpm). Low-latency remote desktop/game streaming. Hardware decoding (VA-API/NVDEC), gamepad passthrough. Hosting from Linux has limitations vs Windows.

alternatives.name: Moonlight + Sunshine
alternatives.notes: Open-source game streaming. Moonlight (client) uses NVIDIA GameStream/Sunshine (host) protocol. Very low latency, 4K HDR, multi-controller support.

alternatives.name: Steam Remote Play
alternatives.notes: Built into Steam. Stream games between PCs on LAN or internet. Good for Steam library, not general desktop streaming.

alternatives.name: pgAdmin 4 (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official pgAdmin for Linux (.deb/.rpm or PGDG repo). PostgreSQL administration: query editor, ERD, server monitoring, backup/restore, import/export, user management. Identical to Windows.

install.note: pgAdmin 4 from the official apt/dnf repo

alternatives.name: pgAdmin 4
alternatives.notes: Most complete PostgreSQL GUI admin tool. pgNow features (query plan visualization, schema diff, data export) covered by pgAdmin + built-in tools.

install.note: pgAdmin 4 from the official apt/dnf repo

alternatives.name: DBeaver
alternatives.notes: Universal DB client with excellent PostgreSQL support. ER diagrams, schema compare, data export/import, spatial data viewer. More polished UI than pgAdmin.

alternatives.name: psql (CLI)
alternatives.notes: PostgreSQL's own CLI. \d commands for schema browsing, \e for query editing, \timing for benchmarking. Most powerful option for experienced DBAs.

alternatives.name: Shotwell
alternatives.notes: GNOME photo manager. Import from camera, organize by date/event/tag, basic editing (crop, red-eye, color adjust, straighten), slideshow, publish to Flickr/Piwigo/Google Photos.

alternatives.name: gThumb
alternatives.notes: Image viewer and organizer. Batch rename/resize/convert, lossless JPEG transforms, comments/tags, print layouts, Web album export. More features than Shotwell.

alternatives.name: digiKam
alternatives.notes: Professional photo management. RAW processing, face detection/recognition, geotagging, advanced search, Lightroom-like workflow. KDE-based but runs everywhere.

alternatives.name: GNOME Loupe
alternatives.notes: Modern GNOME image viewer. GPU-accelerated, touchpad pinch-to-zoom, smooth scrolling. Only viewing, no editing/organizing.

alternatives.name: XnView MP
alternatives.notes: Cross-platform image viewer/manager. 500+ formats, batch processing, metadata editing. Very Windows-like UX. Free for personal use.

applications.name: PostgreSQL
applications.version: N/A (hardcoded inclusion as dependency for pgAdmin)

alternatives.name: PostgreSQL (PGDG repo)
alternatives.notes: PostgreSQL installed from the official PGDG APT repository (apt.postgresql.org). Installed alongside pgAdmin for a full PostgreSQL development environment. Latest version always available.

alternatives.name: n8n
alternatives.notes: Fair-code workflow automation. 400+ integrations, visual editor, webhooks, cron triggers, error handling. Self-hosted or cloud. Best RPA-alternative on Linux.

alternatives.name: cron + systemd timers
alternatives.notes: Native Linux scheduling. cron (time-based job scheduler) + systemd timers (more advanced: calendar events, monotonic timers, randomized delays). Combined with shell scripts for automation.

alternatives.name: AutoKey
alternatives.notes: Desktop automation utility. Trigger Python scripts via hotkeys or abbreviations. Text expansion, keyboard macros, window management. Closest to Power Automate Desktop.

alternatives.name: Huginn
alternatives.notes: IFTTT-like agent system. Create agents that monitor the web, process data, send notifications. Scrapers, email digesters, webhook receivers. Self-hosted, Ruby-based.

alternatives.name: Brasero / K3b
alternatives.notes: CD/DVD/Blu-ray burning suites. Brasero (GNOME), K3b (KDE). Burn ISOs, create data/audio discs, rip CDs, verify burns. Cover PowerISO's disc-burning features.

alternatives.name: AcetoneISO
alternatives.notes: Create/mount/convert ISOs. Supports ISO/BIN/CUE/NRG/MDF/IMG formats. Encrypt/decrypt disc images. Convert CD/DVD to ISO.

alternatives.name: mkisofs / genisoimage + dd
alternatives.notes: `mkisofs -o output.iso /path/to/files/` (create ISO), `dd if=image.iso of=/dev/sr0` (burn to disc). CLI tools that PowerISO wraps.

alternatives.name: PowerShell (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Microsoft PowerShell for Linux. Install via APT repo (packages.microsoft.com). pwsh CLI â€” identical modules, remoting, DSC. Cross-platform scripting.

install.note: or use the Microsoft package repo

alternatives.name: Bash
alternatives.notes: The native Linux shell. Pre-installed. Pipe objects â†’ pipe text. Different paradigm from PowerShell â€” text streams vs structured objects. More native to Linux ecosystem.

alternatives.name: Zsh
alternatives.notes: Extended shell with better interactive features. With Oh My Zsh: plugins, themes, auto-completion, syntax highlighting. Bash compatible but much nicer UX.

alternatives.name: Fish
alternatives.notes: User-friendly shell. Out-of-the-box: syntax highlighting, auto-suggestions (from history), tab completions, web-based configuration. Not POSIX-compatible (different scripting).

alternatives.name: Frog (Text Extractor replacement)
alternatives.notes: TenderOwl Frog: shortcut-triggered OCR, select any screen area â†’ extract text. FOSS, supports 50+ languages via Tesseract. Maps to PowerToys Text Extractor.

alternatives.name: Ulauncher (Run replacement)
alternatives.notes: Application launcher (like macOS Spotlight / PowerToys Run). Search apps, files, web, calculator, system commands. Extensible with Python plugins.

alternatives.name: gpick / KColorChooser (Color Picker)
alternatives.notes: Color picker tools: shortcut â†’ click anywhere on screen â†’ get hex/RGB/HSL value + color palette. Maps to PowerToys Color Picker.

alternatives.name: nautilus-image-converter + ImageMagick (Image Resizer)
alternatives.notes: Right-click in Nautilus file manager â†’ resize/rotate images. ImageMagick `convert` / `mogrify` for CLI batch operations. Maps to PowerToys Image Resizer.

alternatives.name: Barrier / Input Leap (Mouse Without Borders)
alternatives.notes: Software KVM: share one keyboard/mouse across multiple computers (even Windows â†” Linux). Clipboard sharing, drag-and-drop files. Network-based. Maps to PowerToys Mouse Without Borders.

alternatives.name: ibus-typing-booster (Keyboard Manager / Accents)
alternatives.notes: Intelligent input method: predictive typing, compose sequences for accented characters (Ã©, Ã±, Ã¼). Or use Compose key (built-in). Maps to PowerToys Keyboard Manager.

alternatives.name: KMag / GNOME Magnifier (ZoomIt)
alternatives.notes: Screen magnifier. KMag (KDE) / GNOME Magnifier (built-in Super+Alt+8). Or use screen recorder for annotation-free recording. Maps to PowerToys ZoomIt.

alternatives.name: lsof / fuser (File Locksmith)
alternatives.notes: Find which process is locking a file. `lsof /path/to/file` or `fuser -v /path/to/file`. GNOME File Locksmith (GUI) also available. Maps to PowerToys File Locksmith.

alternatives.name: GNOME Shortcuts overlay (Shortcut Guide)
alternatives.notes: Press Super (Windows) key â†’ GNOME shows all keyboard shortcuts. Or `man <application>` or KDE shortcut cheat sheet. Maps to PowerToys Shortcut Guide.

applications.name: Proton VPN (Hotspot Shield / Psiphon Alternative)
applications.version: N/A (best alternative for Hotspot Shield / Psiphon)

alternatives.name: Proton VPN (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Proton VPN Linux client. Free tier (unlimited data, 5 countries). Swiss privacy (audited no-logs), Secure Core (multi-hop), NetShield (ad/malware blocker), kill switch, port forwarding (paid). Replaces both Hotspot Shield and Psiphon.

install.note: No official Linux client; use Proton VPN or OpenVPN instead

alternatives.name: proxychains-ng
alternatives.notes: Force any TCP application through proxy chain. `proxychains4 curl example.com`. SOCKS4/5, HTTP proxies, chaining (strict/dynamic/random). Preload library injects proxying.

alternatives.name: redsocks
alternatives.notes: Transparent TCP redirector through SOCKS/HTTP proxy. Uses iptables/nftables to redirect traffic. System-wide proxying without app modification.

alternatives.name: torsocks
alternatives.notes: Force application through Tor. `torsocks wget example.com`. Preload library. Tor-specific (SOCKS5 with DNS over Tor). More secure than generic proxychains.

alternatives.name: Proton VPN
alternatives.notes: Swiss-based VPN with free tier (unlimited data, 5 countries). Strong privacy (audited no-logs), Secure Core, kill switch. Native Linux GUI. Same censorship-circumvention use case.

install.note: Proton VPN app via the official apt/dnf repo

alternatives.name: Tor Browser
alternatives.notes: Anonymous browsing via Tor network with obfs4 bridges. Built-in censorship circumvention (meek, snowflake, obfs4 transports). Best for anti-censorship browsing.

alternatives.name: Psiphon (Linux CLI)
alternatives.notes: Psiphon has a Linux CLI client. Same network, same protocols. No official GUI for Linux.

alternatives.name: Mullvad VPN
alternatives.notes: Anonymous accounts (cash/crypto), WireGuard, port forwarding, bridge/obfuscation modes for restrictive networks. Strong anti-censorship option.

alternatives.name: OpenSSH (native)
alternatives.notes: `ssh user@host` â€” the native way. No separate SSH client needed on Linux. Saved connections in ~/.ssh/config with aliases, keys, port forwarding, proxy commands.

alternatives.name: PuTTY (Linux)
alternatives.notes: PuTTY is packaged for Linux (`apt install putty`). Same UI as Windows version. Useful if you have PuTTY saved sessions you want to import.

alternatives.name: Terminator / Tilix
alternatives.notes: Tiling terminal emulators. Split panes, broadcast input to multiple terminals, profiles for appearance/behavior. Use with native ssh.

alternatives.name: python3 (system)
alternatives.notes: Python is pre-installed on every Linux distro. `python3` command. System Python serves OS tools â€” install user tools in venvs or use pyenv.

alternatives.name: pyenv
alternatives.notes: Per-user Python version manager. Install/switch between multiple Python versions (3.8 through 3.14+). Poetry/pipenv compatible. Don't mess with system Python.

alternatives.name: Anaconda / Miniconda
alternatives.notes: Python + scientific packages + conda env manager. Excellent for data science. conda-forge has larger package ecosystem than PyPI for native binaries.

alternatives.name: QUICKfind (Web)
alternatives.notes: CABI QUICKfind is a taxonomic search and identification tool for crop pests and diseases. The server component serves web-based access; check with CABI for browser-based access or API alternatives that work on Linux.

alternatives.name: Docker + QUICKfind
alternatives.notes: The QUICKfind server runs as a Java/Tomcat application. If CABI provides a Linux-compatible distribution, it can be containerized with Docker for Linux deployment. Verify licensing with CABI.

alternatives.name: R (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official R for Linux. CRAN repos pre-configured. All CRAN packages compile natively (no Rtools needed). Same scripts, same output. Faster on Linux for many operations.

alternatives.name: RealVNC Viewer (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official RealVNC Viewer for Linux. .deb/.rpm downloads from RealVNC. Proprietary but free for viewer. VNC, audio, printing, file transfer, chat.

alternatives.name: Remmina
alternatives.notes: Multi-protocol remote desktop client. VNC plugin with SSH tunneling, different encodings, scaling. Works with any VNC server including RealVNC.

alternatives.name: TigerVNC Viewer
alternatives.notes: High-performance VNC client. Optimized for 3D/GPU-accelerated desktops. Tighter encoding for slow links.

alternatives.name: TestDisk + PhotoRec
alternatives.notes: PhotoRec recovers 480+ file formats (photos, docs, videos, archives) from memory cards, HDDs, SSDs. TestDisk recovers lost partitions. Far more capable than Recuva.

alternatives.name: R-Linux
alternatives.notes: GUI file recovery from R-Studio. Free for EXT filesystems. Familiar Recuva-like interface (scan, preview, recover).

alternatives.name: extundelete
alternatives.notes: Undelete files from ext3/ext4 partitions. Requires unmounting first (or remount read-only). Success rate depends on time since deletion.

alternatives.name: Reverso (Web)
alternatives.notes: reverso.net in browser. Translation, context, grammar check, synonyms, conjugation. Full functionality. PWA-capable.

alternatives.name: GoldenDict / GoldenDict-ng
alternatives.notes: Offline dictionary with multiple formats. Scan popup translation, Wikipedia/Freedict integration. Good for offline use but different from Reverso's context-rich approach.

alternatives.name: Crow Translate
alternatives.notes: Qt-based translator app using Google/Bing/Yandex Translate APIs. System tray, hotkey translate, text-to-speech. Lightweight desktop wrapper for online services.

alternatives.name: Dialect
alternatives.notes: GNOME translation app. LibreTranslate (self-hosted) or Google Translate backend. Clean GNOME UI, text-to-speech, clipboard integration.

alternatives.name: RStudio (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official RStudio Desktop for Linux (.deb/.rpm). Identical IDE: script editor, console, environment/history, plots, package manager, Git, R Markdown. All features.

alternatives.name: Positron
alternatives.notes: Posit's next-gen data science IDE (VS Code-based). R + Python support, Quarto, better Copilot integration. Early-stage but promising replacement.

alternatives.name: Ventoy
alternatives.notes: Multi-boot USB creator. Install to USB once â†’ drag-and-drop ISOs to boot from. Supports 1000+ distros, Windows ISOs, UEFI/Legacy, persistence, exFAT/NTFS/ext4. Much better than Rufus.

alternatives.name: balenaEtcher
alternatives.notes: Cross-platform USB flasher. Simple 3-step UI: select image â†’ select drive â†’ flash. Validation after write. AppImage for Linux.

alternatives.name: GNOME Disks (gnome-disk-utility)
alternatives.notes: Built-in GNOME disk tool. Can restore disk images to USB: select drive â†’ Restore Disk Image â†’ choose ISO. No special bootable-USB magic â€” just raw write.

alternatives.name: Fedora Media Writer
alternatives.notes: Fedora's official USB creator. Downloads ISOs automatically, verifies checksum, writes to USB. Cross-platform (Linux, Windows, macOS).

alternatives.name: dd (CLI)
alternatives.notes: `sudo dd if=image.iso of=/dev/sdX bs=4M status=progress` â€” low-level block write. No validation, no bootable magic. Dangerous if wrong `of=` device. Always available.

alternatives.name: Samsung Account (Web)
alternatives.notes: account.samsung.com works fully in any Linux browser. Manage Samsung devices, subscriptions, cloud services, and account settings. Samsung Galaxy Buds, SmartThings, and Find My Mobile all have web interfaces.

alternatives.name: SmartThings (Web)
alternatives.notes: Samsung SmartThings has a web dashboard at account.smartthings.com for managing IoT devices. Works in any browser on Linux.

alternatives.name: smartmontools
alternatives.notes: SMART health monitoring: `smartctl -a /dev/nvme0n1`. Temperature, wear level, bad blocks, lifetime writes, self-tests. GNOME Disks shows SMART in GUI.

alternatives.name: nvme-cli
alternatives.notes: NVMe management: `nvme list`, `nvme id-ctrl /dev/nvme0`, `nvme smart-log /dev/nvme0`. Format, sanitize, firmware commit. Low-level NVMe admin.

alternatives.name: fwupd
alternatives.notes: Firmware updates via LVFS (Linux Vendor Firmware Service). Samsung SSD firmware updates delivered through fwupd. `fwupdmgr get-devices` â†’ `fwupdmgr update`.

alternatives.name: SAS (Linux)
alternatives.notes: SAS has official Linux builds. Most SAS Enterprise environments run on Linux servers. Full SAS/BASE, STAT, ETS, etc.

alternatives.name: R + RStudio
alternatives.notes: R provides SAS-equivalent statistical power. Many SAS-to-R migration paths exist. More modern, free, larger community. Different syntax (SAS language vs R).

applications.name: SketchUp
applications.linuxAvailability: Not Available; Native Alternative; Available as WebApp

alternatives.name: SketchUp Free (Web)
alternatives.notes: app.sketchup.com â€” Trimble's free web version. Full 3D modeling, 3D Warehouse access, cloud sync. Slightly limited vs Pro but covers core features. Works in any browser.

alternatives.name: Blender
alternatives.notes: Full 3D creation suite. Much more powerful than SketchUp but steeper learning curve. Mesh modeling, architectural visualization (Archipack add-on), rendering (Cycles/Eevee). Overkill for simple modeling. FreeCAD is closer for architectural workflow.

alternatives.name: FreeCAD (Arch/BIM workbench)
alternatives.notes: Parametric 3D CAD. BIM/Arch workbench for architectural modeling (walls, windows, roofs). Constraint-based sketching â€” different paradigm from SketchUp's push/pull but more precise for engineering.

alternatives.name: Sweet Home 3D
alternatives.notes: Interior design app. Drag-and-drop furniture, 2D plan â†’ 3D view, furniture library. Simpler than SketchUp but purpose-built for home/interior planning.

alternatives.name: Flameshot + Ksnip
alternatives.notes: Flameshot: fast screenshot + annotation (arrows, text, blur). Ksnip: scrolling capture, numbered steps, delayed capture, FTP upload. Combined they cover Snagit's full feature set.

alternatives.name: ksnip
alternatives.notes: Closest single-tool Snagit alternative. Rectangular/fullscreen/active window/delay capture, extensive annotation (numbered steps, arrows, boxes, blur), FTP/Imgur upload, scrolling capture. Flatpak.

alternatives.name: Spectacle + Gwenview
alternatives.notes: KDE combo: Spectacle (screenshot with annotation) + Gwenview (basic image editing). KDE-native, no extra install on Plasma desktops.

alternatives.name: Flameshot
alternatives.notes: Select area â†’ annotate with arrows/text/boxes/blur/pin â†’ copy/save/upload to Imgur. Configurable shortcut (PrtScrn). Best snipping tool on any platform.

alternatives.name: Spectacle
alternatives.notes: KDE screenshot tool with annotation support. Rectangular/fullscreen/active window/delay capture. Export, copy to clipboard, annotations.

alternatives.name: GNOME Screenshot (built-in)
alternatives.notes: PrtScrn (full screen), Alt+PrtScrn (window), Shift+PrtScrn (area select). Built into GNOME Shell. Saves to ~/Pictures/Screenshots/. No annotation, but fast and always available.

alternatives.name: OpenSSH + terminal
alternatives.notes: Snoopy is a Windows SSH/tabbed terminal utility. On Linux, the built-in terminal (GNOME Terminal/Konsole) + OpenSSH provides all Snoopy functionality natively. No separate tool needed.

alternatives.name: Tabby
alternatives.notes: Modern cross-platform terminal with SSH client, SFTP, config sync.

alternatives.name: Terminator
alternatives.notes: Feature-rich terminal with tiling, broadcast input, profiles.

alternatives.name: FreeCAD
alternatives.notes: Parametric 3D modeler. Part Design, Sketcher, Assembly (A3/A4 workbenches), FEM, CAM. FOSS alternative but much less polished than SolidWorks.

alternatives.name: Solvespace
alternatives.notes: Parametric 3D CAD with constraint solver. Extremely small (~6MB), export STEP/STL/DXF. Good constraint-based sketcher but minimal features.

alternatives.name: Onshape (Web)
alternatives.notes: The best web-based CAD for SolidWorks users (founded by former SolidWorks team). Full parametric 3D, assembly, drawings. Professional, collaborative, browser-based.

alternatives.name: BricsCAD Mechanical
alternatives.notes: Native Linux 3D CAD with mechanical tools. Sheet metal, assembly, BOM. Native DWG format. Commercial SolidWorks alternative on Linux.

alternatives.name: GNOME Sound Recorder
alternatives.notes: Simple audio recorder for GNOME. Select mic â†’ record â†’ saves to ~/Recordings in Opus/MP3/FLAC/WAV. Clean UI, just works. Pre-installed on Ubuntu.

alternatives.name: Audacity
alternatives.notes: Full-featured audio editor. Multi-track recording, effects (noise reduction, EQ, compression), spectrogram view, plugin support (VST/LADSPA/Nyquist). Overkill for simple recording.

alternatives.name: RecApp
alternatives.notes: Simple GTK audio recorder. Flatpak, saves in Opus/FLAC/MP3/WAV. Timer-based recording, auto-save. Cleaner than Audacity for simple recording.

alternatives.name: CoolerControl / Coolero
alternatives.notes: GUI for liquidctl â€” controls AIO coolers, fan controllers, RGB (Corsair, NZXT, ASUS, etc.). Fan curves, temperature monitoring, pump control. Modern Linux fan control.

install.note: CoolerControl from gitlab releases; or lm-sensors + fancontrol

alternatives.name: fancontrol / thinkfan
alternatives.notes: lm-sensors' fancontrol daemon for PWM fan control. Configure fan curves based on temperature sensors. thinkfan for ThinkPad laptops.

alternatives.name: CoreCtrl
alternatives.notes: AMD GPU/CPU control center. Fan curves, overclocking, power profiles, voltage control. For AMD hardware specifically.

alternatives.name: Spotify (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Spotify for Linux (Snap/Flatpak/APT). Identical experience â€” library, playlists, podcasts, Connect (cast to devices). Cloud-synced.

alternatives.name: SpotPlayer (Linux)
alternatives.notes: SpotPlayer has a native Linux build (Ubuntu/.deb) from spotplayer.ir. Same functionality as Windows version.

applications.name: SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS)
applications.linuxAvailability: Not Available; Native Alternative; Linux Docker

alternatives.name: DBeaver
alternatives.notes: Universal DB client with SQL Server support. Query editor (IntelliSense), ER diagrams, schema browsing, data editor, export/import. Best SSMS alternative on Linux.

alternatives.name: Azure Data Studio
alternatives.notes: Microsoft's own cross-platform DB tool (VS Code-based). SQL Server Notebooks, charting, extensions, terminal. Modern, lightweight vs SSMS.

alternatives.name: VS Code + mssql extension
alternatives.notes: VS Code with Microsoft's official 'SQL Server (mssql)' extension. Query execution, IntelliSense, results grid. Lightweight approach if you already use VS Code.

alternatives.name: sqlcmd + mssql-cli
alternatives.notes: Microsoft's CLI tools for SQL Server. sqlcmd (official, cross-platform), mssql-cli (interactive with auto-completion). Good for scripting/automation.

alternatives.name: Stata (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Stata for Linux (.tar.gz). Same commands, same .dta files, same do-files. Install via StataCorp download. Industry-standard econometrics/epidemiology tool.

alternatives.name: R + RStudio
alternatives.notes: R with RStudio covers Stata's domain well (econometrics, biostatistics). Packages: plm (panel data), fixest (fixed effects), ivreg (instrumental variables), survival (survival analysis). Free and more flexible.

alternatives.name: Steam (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Steam for Linux. Native Linux games + Proton (Valve's Wine fork) for Windows games. Steam Play, cloud saves, Remote Play, VR (HTC Vive/Valve Index). Thousands of Windows games run via Proton.

alternatives.name: Lutris
alternatives.notes: Open gaming platform. Manage Steam/GOG/Epic/Origin/Battle.net games + emulators + Windows games via Wine in one place. Community install scripts automate complex setups.

alternatives.name: Heroic Games Launcher
alternatives.notes: Open-source Epic Games + GOG launcher. Browse library, download/install/launch games with Wine/Proton. Clean modern UI.

alternatives.name: GNOME Sticky Notes (sticky)
alternatives.notes: GNOME extension: put sticky notes on desktop, resizable/colored, always-on-top, pin to dash. Simple like MS Sticky Notes. Combine with Joplin for cloud-synced rich notes.

alternatives.name: Joplin
alternatives.notes: Note-taking app with cloud sync (OneDrive/Nextcloud/Dropbox/WebDAV/Joplin Cloud). Markdown notes, to-dos, tags, notebooks, web clipper, E2E encryption. Much more powerful than Sticky Notes.

alternatives.name: Xpad
alternatives.notes: Simple sticky note app for GTK. Multiple pads, customizable fonts/colors, tray icon. Very lightweight, close to the Windows Sticky Notes simplicity.

alternatives.name: Obsidian
alternatives.notes: Knowledge base on local Markdown files. Graph view, backlinks, plugins, themes. Sync via Obsidian Sync ($) or any file sync (Nextcloud/Git). Very powerful but different paradigm from sticky notes.

alternatives.name: Perl (native)
alternatives.notes: Perl is pre-installed on every Linux distro. `perl -v`. CPAN modules via `cpan` or `cpanminus`. Strawberry Perl was the Windows port â€” this is the original.

alternatives.name: TeamViewer (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official TeamViewer for Linux (.deb/.rpm). Remote desktop, file transfer, VPN, meeting. Free for personal use. Same features as Windows.

alternatives.name: RustDesk
alternatives.notes: Open-source remote desktop (TeamViewer/AnyDesk alternative). Self-hostable server, no relay dependencies. Almost identical UX.

alternatives.name: Telegram Desktop (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Telegram Desktop for Linux (APT/Flatpak). Identical experience: chats, channels, voice/video calls, bots, stickers, secret chats (E2E). Fully cloud-synced.

alternatives.name: Terminator (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Feature-rich terminal emulator. Tiling (split/group terminals), broadcast input to all/group, profiles, plugins. Installed via APT.

applications.name: Typing Master / Typing Tutor
applications.linuxAvailability: Not Available; Native Alternative; Available as WebApp

alternatives.name: Klavaro
alternatives.notes: Touch typing tutor. Supports 30+ keyboard layouts, adaptive lessons, speed/accuracy tracking, progress charts. Native Linux app, closest TypingMaster alternative.

alternatives.name: Tipp10
alternatives.notes: Cross-platform typing tutor. Intelligent error analysis (repeats your problem characters), custom lessons, detailed statistics. Free (FOSS) version available.

alternatives.name: Monkeytype (Web)
alternatives.notes: Modern browser-based typing test. Configurable word lists (quotes, code, custom), themes, stats tracking, online leaderboard. monkeytype.com.

alternatives.name: keybr.com (Web)
alternatives.notes: Smart typing tutor. Uses AI to identify weak keys and generates focused practice. Track progress with detailed heatmaps. No install needed.

alternatives.name: Typora (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Typora for Linux (.deb/.tar.gz). WYSIWYG Markdown editor â€” type and see rendered output immediately. Math (LaTeX), diagrams (Mermaid/Sequence), code fences, themes. One-time purchase.

alternatives.name: MarkText
alternatives.notes: Open-source Typora clone. Same WYSIWYG Markdown editing, themes, focus mode, export to PDF/HTML. Electron-based. Very close to Typora's UX but completely free.

alternatives.name: Zettlr
alternatives.notes: Markdown editor for academic writing. Zettelkasten, citations (Zotero/JabRef), LaTeX math, presentations. More features than Typora but different focus (academic/technical writing).

alternatives.name: Ghostwriter
alternatives.notes: Distraction-free Markdown editor (KDE). Live preview, focus mode, themes, Hemingway mode (disable backspace), export to many formats. Native KDE app â€” very fast.

alternatives.name: Native Linux
alternatives.notes: You are already on Linux â€” WSL is a Windows subsystem to run Linux. No WSL needed when running Linux directly.

install.note: Not applicable - you are already running Linux

alternatives.name: AcetoneISO
alternatives.notes: Create/mount/convert ISOs, edit ISO contents, mount multiple formats (ISO/BIN/NRG/MDF/IMG). Closest UltraISO alternative on Linux.

alternatives.name: ISO Master
alternatives.notes: GUI ISO editor. Open ISO, add/remove/extract files, edit boot record, save modified ISO. Simple UltraISO-like workflow.

alternatives.name: mkisofs / xorriso
alternatives.notes: CLI ISO creation/editing. `xorriso -dev output.iso -add /files/` â€” powerful scripting for ISO manipulation. The underlying tools UltraISO wraps.

alternatives.name: VirtualBox (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Oracle VirtualBox for Linux via Oracle's own APT repo (download.virtualbox.org). Guest Additions, snapshots, virtual networking, USB passthrough. Cross-platform VM manager.

alternatives.name: KVM + virt-manager
alternatives.notes: Linux native virtualization (built into kernel). Near-native performance, PCIe passthrough, snapshots. Better performance than VirtualBox, no extra kernel modules needed.

alternatives.name: .NET SDK + VS Code
alternatives.notes: .NET SDK CLI (`dotnet build`, `dotnet test`, `dotnet publish`) + VS Code with C# Dev Kit extension. Covers most .NET development scenarios.

install.note: also install the build toolchain (build-essential / @development-tools) and dotnet-sdk

alternatives.name: JetBrains Rider
alternatives.notes: Full .NET IDE for Linux. Resharper-level code analysis, database tools, Docker, Unity/Unreal support. Best VS replacement for .NET on Linux. Paid (free for OSS/students).

alternatives.name: Mono + MSBuild
alternatives.notes: Open-source .NET Framework implementation. `msbuild`, `xbuild`. For legacy .NET Framework projects. Modern .NET uses .NET SDK (above).

alternatives.name: GCC/Clang + Make/CMake
alternatives.notes: C/C++ native build toolchain. Gcc/clang (compilers), make/cmake (build systems). The native Linux way for C/C++ development â€” Visual Studio Build Tools' original domain.

alternatives.name: Visual Studio Code (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official VS Code for Linux (.deb/.rpm or Flatpak). Settings Sync via GitHub/Microsoft account. Extensions, themes, keybindings â€” everything identical.

repo.apt:
install -d -m 0755 /etc/apt/keyrings
curl -fsSL https://packages.microsoft.com/keys/microsoft.asc | gpg --batch --yes --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/microsoft.gpg
echo "deb [arch=amd64,arm64 signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/microsoft.gpg] https://packages.microsoft.com/repos/code stable main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/vscode.list

alternatives.name: VS Codium
alternatives.notes: Community build of VS Code without Microsoft telemetry/branding. Uses open-vsx extension marketplace. Fully compatible with VS Code settings.

alternatives.name: VS Code Insiders
alternatives.notes: Nightly VS Code builds. Access to newest features, extensions can be ahead of stable API. Can install alongside stable.

alternatives.name: JetBrains Fleet
alternatives.notes: JetBrains' lightweight editor/IDE. Smart completion, refactoring, remote development. Freemium â€” basic editor free, advanced IDE features paid.

alternatives.name: VMware Workstation Pro (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official VMware Workstation Pro for Linux. Now free for personal use. .bundle installer from Broadcom. Full feature parity: snapshots, cloning, virtual networks, GPU passthrough.

install.note: VMware Workstation Pro .bundle (free for personal use)

alternatives.name: KVM + virt-manager
alternatives.notes: Linux native virtualization (KVM kernel module + QEMU + libvirt). Virt-manager (GUI) or virt-viewer. Near-native performance, PCIe passthrough, snapshots. No extra kernel module needed.

alternatives.name: VirtualBox
alternatives.notes: Oracle VirtualBox. Cross-platform, simpler than KVM for basic VMs. Guest Additions for seamless mouse/display/shared folders. Slower than KVM (no kernel-level acceleration by default).

alternatives.name: GNOME Boxes
alternatives.notes: Simple GNOME VM manager (uses QEMU/KVM underneath). Auto-download ISOs, express installation. Good for quick VMs, not for advanced features (snapshots, VLANs, PCIe passthrough).

alternatives.name: GNOME Weather
alternatives.notes: GNOME weather app with automatic location detection. Hourly, daily, and weekly forecasts, multiple locations. Clean GNOME design. Pre-installed on Ubuntu.

alternatives.name: KWeather
alternatives.notes: KDE weather app. Multiple weather providers, severe weather alerts, Plasma panel widget. Beautiful charts and maps.

alternatives.name: Meteo
alternatives.notes: GTK4 weather app using Open-Meteo API. Modern UI with animated weather conditions, forecasts, maps. Flatpak.

alternatives.name: WhatsApp Web (PWA)
alternatives.notes: web.whatsapp.com as PWA in Chrome/Edge/Brave. Desktop notifications, keyboard shortcuts, voice/video calls (in supported browsers). No native install needed.

alternatives.name: ZapZap
alternatives.notes: Native Linux WhatsApp client (Flatpak). Web wrapper with native desktop features: system tray, spell check, notifications, custom themes. More integrated than PWA.

alternatives.name: Whatsie
alternatives.notes: Simple WhatsApp Web wrapper. System tray, unread count badge, dark mode toggle. Lightweight Electron-based wrapper.

alternatives.name: QDirStat
alternatives.notes: Qt-based disk usage analyzer with treemap visualization. Cache-aware (squashfs/snap), cleanup scripts, custom actions. The closest WinDirStat port to Linux.

alternatives.name: baobab (GNOME Disk Usage Analyzer)
alternatives.notes: GNOME disk usage analyzer. Ring chart and treemap visualization, scan home/folder/whole filesystem, real-time scanning. Clean, simple UI.

alternatives.name: Filelight
alternatives.notes: KDE disk usage analyzer. Radial sunburst chart (interactive â€” click segments to drill down). Fast, beautiful. Integrates with Dolphin file manager.

alternatives.name: ncdu
alternatives.notes: NCurses disk usage analyzer (terminal-based). `ncdu /` â€” browse directories, see sizes, delete files. Fast (parallel scanning via `ncdu -t`). Good for SSH/headless.

alternatives.name: GNOME Calculator
alternatives.notes: Pre-installed on GNOME desktops. Basic, Advanced, Financial, Programming modes. Keyboard/numlock support, history. Clean, functional.

alternatives.name: KCalc
alternatives.notes: KDE calculator. Scientific, statistics, numeral system, trigonometry. Customizable precision, constants, memory functions. Pre-installed on KDE.

alternatives.name: Qalculate! (GTK/Qt)
alternatives.notes: Powerful calculator with symbolic algebra. Units, variables, functions, plotting, RPN mode, interval arithmetic. The most powerful calculator on any platform.

alternatives.name: SpeedCrunch
alternatives.notes: Keyboard-driven scientific calculator. History, syntax highlighting, auto-completion, 50-digit precision, constants library, user functions. Very fast input-focused workflow.

alternatives.name: Cheese
alternatives.notes: GNOME webcam app. Photo capture, video recording, burst mode, countdown timer, cheesy effects. Simple and works with all UVC webcams.

alternatives.name: GNOME Snapshot
alternatives.notes: Modern GNOME camera app. Photo capture with camera controls (zoom, flash, timer). GPU-accelerated pipeline. Flatpak on newer GNOME.

alternatives.name: Kamoso
alternatives.notes: KDE webcam app. Photo/video capture, gallery, upload to social media. Works with UVC cameras.

alternatives.name: Guvcview
alternatives.notes: GTK UVC webcam viewer. Shows all camera controls (brightness, contrast, gain, focus, etc.). Photo/video capture, more control options than Cheese.

alternatives.name: Simple Scan (Document Scanner)
alternatives.notes: GNOME scanning app. Auto-detect scanner (SANE), single/multi-page scans, crop/rotate, save as PDF/JPG/PNG. Pre-installed on Ubuntu. Excellent scan quality. Covers the Scan half.

alternatives.name: gscan2pdf
alternatives.notes: Scanner to PDF with OCR (Tesseract). Multi-page scans, deskew, cleanup, compress, metadata. More advanced than Simple Scan â€” better for document archival.

alternatives.name: Skanlite / Skanpage
alternatives.notes: KDE scanning apps. Skanlite (simple), Skanpage (advanced with OCR). Auto-detect, multi-page, various formats.

alternatives.name: HylaFAX + CAPI4Hylafax
alternatives.notes: Enterprise-grade fax server for Linux. Send/receive faxes via modem/CAPI/ISDN. Covers the Fax half. Complex setup â€” fax is a legacy technology with limited Linux options.

alternatives.name: OpenShot
alternatives.notes: Beginner-friendly video editor. Drag-and-drop timeline, titles, transitions, effects, audio tracks, export to many formats. The perfect Movie Maker replacement â€” designed for the same audience.

alternatives.name: Pitivi
alternatives.notes: GNOME video editor. Clean timeline, good for simple edits (trim, arrange, transitions). GStreamer-based â€” very reliable playback. Simpler than OpenShot.

alternatives.name: Kdenlive
alternatives.notes: More powerful than Movie Maker but still approachable. Multi-track, proxy editing, keyframes, effects. Good upgrade path as you grow beyond Movie Maker's capabilities.

alternatives.name: GNOME Text Editor / gedit
alternatives.notes: Pre-installed on most distros. Syntax highlighting, tabs, spell check, line numbers, search/replace, file browser sidebar. Much more than Notepad.

alternatives.name: Kate
alternatives.notes: KDE advanced text editor. LSP client (code completion), multi-cursor, vi input mode, terminal panel, sessions, projects. More like a lightweight IDE.

alternatives.name: Leafpad / Mousepad
alternatives.notes: Ultra-lightweight text editors. No syntax highlighting, no tabs â€” truly Notepad-equivalent in simplicity. Leafpad for GTK2, Mousepad for XFCE.

alternatives.name: micro
alternatives.notes: Terminal-based editor with common shortcuts (Ctrl+S save, Ctrl+Q quit, Ctrl+Z undo). Syntax highlighting, mouse support, split panes. Good for terminal-only workflows.

alternatives.name: GNOME Image Viewer (Loupe)
alternatives.notes: Modern GNOME image viewer. GPU-accelerated, touchpad pinch-to-zoom, smooth scrolling. Minimalist â€” just viewing, no editing. Pre-installed on Ubuntu 24.04+.

alternatives.name: Eye of GNOME (eog)
alternatives.notes: Classic GNOME image viewer (pre-installed on older Ubuntu). Slideshow, rotate, set as wallpaper. Simple and fast â€” just view images without clutter.

alternatives.name: Gwenview
alternatives.notes: KDE image viewer. Browse folders, thumbnails, basic editing (crop, resize, red-eye, color adjust), slideshow, share via KDE Connect. Pre-installed on KDE.

alternatives.name: GNOME Software / KDE Discover
alternatives.notes: Your distro's app store. Supports APT packages + Flatpaks + Snaps + firmware updates. Installed by default. Browse, search, install, update, remove â€” just like Store.

alternatives.name: Flathub (web)
alternatives.notes: flathub.org â€” the largest Linux app store (2000+ apps). Sandboxed Flatpaks, run on any distro. Verified badges for official apps, user reviews, screenshots.

alternatives.name: Snap Store
alternatives.notes: Canonical's Snap Store. Auto-updating, sandboxed, CLI + web store (snapcraft.io). Ships with Ubuntu, available on all distros.

alternatives.name: GNOME Console / GNOME Terminal
alternatives.notes: Pre-installed. GNOME Console (newer, adaptive, headerbar) or GNOME Terminal (classic). Tabs, profiles, transparency, custom fonts, unlimited scrollback.

alternatives.name: Konsole
alternatives.notes: KDE terminal. Split view, profiles, SSH manager, notifications on activity/silence, bookmarks, built-in color schemes. The most feature-rich native Linux terminal.

alternatives.name: Tabby
alternatives.notes: Cross-platform terminal (Electron). Config sync via cloud, SSH client, serial terminal, SFTP file manager, themes, plugins. Closest UX to Windows Terminal.

alternatives.name: Ghostty
alternatives.notes: GPU-accelerated terminal emulator. Native (Zig), extremely fast, split panes, ligature support, Kitty graphics protocol. Most modern terminal on any platform.

alternatives.name: Alacritty / Kitty / WezTerm
alternatives.notes: GPU-accelerated terminal emulators. Minimal UI, configured via text files (YAML/Lua). Extremely fast rendering. For power users who want pure performance.

alternatives.name: Tilix / Terminator
alternatives.notes: Tiling terminal emulators. Split panes, broadcast input to multiple terminals, drag-and-drop reordering, custom titles/profiles. Great for sysadmin work.

alternatives.name: WindTerm (Linux)
alternatives.notes: WindTerm Linux build (.deb from GitHub). Fast SSH/Telnet/Serial client with built-in file manager (SFTP), session management, X11 forwarding.

alternatives.name: Wine (winehq-stable)
alternatives.notes: Windows compatibility layer. Required for running Notepad++, WinRAR, and IDM (Internet Download Manager) under Wine. Installed via WineHQ APT repository with i386 architecture support.

alternatives.name: p7zip / 7-Zip
alternatives.notes: p7zip / p7zip-full packages. CLI: `7z x archive.rar` (extract), `7z a archive.7z files/` (create 7z/zip). The gold-standard archiver. Handles RAR extraction via p7zip-rar plugin. `unrar` / `unar` also available.

alternatives.name: PeaZip
alternatives.notes: GUI archive manager for Linux. 200+ formats (including RAR extraction), strong encryption, file splitting, checksums, secure delete. Most WinRAR-like GUI experience.

alternatives.name: Ark / File Roller
alternatives.notes: Pre-installed archive managers for KDE/GNOME. Right-click â†’ Extract / Compress. Uses p7zip unrar underneath. Good for basic archive operations.

alternatives.name: WinRAR (Wine)
alternatives.notes: Run the actual Windows WinRAR under Wine. Works very well â€” identical UI and RAR creation. Use native alternatives for better system integration.

alternatives.name: PeaZip
alternatives.notes: Full-featured GUI archive manager. 200+ formats, strong encryption (AES-256), password manager, secure delete, checksums, file splitting. Most complete WinZip alternative.

alternatives.name: Ark / File Roller
alternatives.notes: Built-in archive manager for KDE/GNOME. Right-click â†’ Extract/Compress. ZIP, 7z, tar.gz, tar.xz, RAR (extract). Good for everyday use.

alternatives.name: p7zip
alternatives.notes: `7z a -tzip archive.zip files/` â€” CLI ZIP creation with best compression ratio. Integrates with file manager context menus.

alternatives.name: wkhtmltopdf (Linux)
alternatives.notes: `apt install wkhtmltopdf` â€” HTML to PDF/image converter using Qt WebKit. Same tool, same CLI. Note: project is unmaintained, consider Puppeteer/Playwright for modern HTMLâ†’PDF.

alternatives.name: Puppeteer / Playwright
alternatives.notes: Modern headless Chrome/Chromium for HTMLâ†’PDF. `npx puppeteer browsers install chrome && node script.js`. Much better rendering (full CSS3/JS), maintained, but heavier.

alternatives.name: WeasyPrint
alternatives.notes: Python HTML/CSS â†’ PDF converter. No JavaScript, pure layout engine. Fast and lightweight. Good for simple static HTMLâ†’PDF.

alternatives.name: Blender (Sculpt Mode)
alternatives.notes: Blender's sculpt mode: dynamic topology, multiresolution modifier, 40+ brushes, masking, face sets, cloth brush, pose brush, remesh. Very capable â€” used professionally for sculpting now. ZBrush still leads in very high poly counts.

alternatives.name: Nomad Sculpt (Android via Waydroid)
alternatives.notes: Mobile sculpting app (like ZBrush CoreMini). Run Android version via Waydroid container. Good for quick organic sculpts. Not a full ZBrush replacement.

alternatives.name: Zoom Workplace (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Official Zoom for Linux (.deb/.rpm/Flatpak). Video/audio/screen sharing, virtual backgrounds, breakout rooms, whiteboard, AI Companion. All features work on Linux. Minor: virtual background without green screen requires CPU (no GPU background replacement yet on Linux).

alternatives.name: Rhythmbox
alternatives.notes: GNOME music player and organizer. Library management, playlists, podcasts, internet radio, CD ripping, Last.fm scrobbling, DAAP sharing. Pre-installed on Ubuntu.

alternatives.name: Lollypop
alternatives.notes: Modern GNOME music player. Album art-based browsing, party mode, lyrics, artist bio, Last.fm/TuneIn integration, MTP sync. Beautiful Material Design-inspired UI.

alternatives.name: Elisa
alternatives.notes: KDE music player. Clean UI, indexed music library, playlist management, radio. Integrates with Plasma desktop (media controls, notifications).

alternatives.name: VLC Media Player
alternatives.notes: Universal media player also works as music player. Playlist manager, equalizer, internet radio/podcast, DLNA streaming. Plays every audio format.

alternatives.name: Audacious
alternatives.notes: Winamp-like music player. Classic Winamp skin support, equalizer with presets, visualization plugins, gapless playback. Lightweight, focused on music playback.

alternatives.name: Strawberry
alternatives.notes: Fork of Clementine/Clementine. Music library, playlists, Last.fm, smart/dynamic playlists, lyrics, artist info, music visualization. Advanced music player for heavy libraries.

alternatives.name: qBittorrent
alternatives.notes: Best-in-class open-source BitTorrent client. Native Qt UI, no ads, built-in search engine, RSS feed reader, sequential downloading.

alternatives.name: Transmission
alternatives.notes: Lightweight, minimalist BitTorrent client. Pre-installed on many distros. Excellent web and CLI interfaces for headless servers.

alternatives.name: Deluge
alternatives.notes: Plugin-based architecture, client-server model ideal for seedbox setups. Thin client can connect to remote daemon.

alternatives.name: KTorrent
alternatives.notes: KDE-native BitTorrent client. Feature-rich with bandwidth scheduling, IP filtering, and web interface plugin.

alternatives.name: aria2
alternatives.notes: CLI-only multi-protocol download utility (HTTP/HTTPS, FTP, BitTorrent, Metalink). Ideal for scripting and headless environments.

applications.name: NordVPN
applications.linuxAvailability: Official native app (apt/dnf repo + install script)

alternatives.name: NordVPN for Linux
alternatives.notes: Official NordVPN client. Subscription required (paid service); the client itself is a free download.

install.note: Install via NordVPN's official script: sh <(curl -sSf https://downloads.nordcdn.com/apps/linux/install.sh)  -- or add their apt/dnf repo. Requires a NordVPN subscription to log in.

alternatives.name: Miniconda (Linux)
alternatives.notes: Minimal conda installer (Python + conda). Same conda environments as on Windows.

install.note: Download the Miniconda installer for your architecture and run: bash Miniconda3-latest-Linux-*.sh
